Title: How to Boot Up Your Computer Introduction: Booting up your computer is the process of turning it on and loading the operating system (OS) and other necessary software. This guide will walk you through the steps to boot up your computer correctly and ensure its optimal performance. By following these instructions, you'll be able to start using your computer confidently and efficiently. Step 1: Plug in Your Computer Plug your computer into a power outlet using the appropriate power cable. Key tip: Always double-check that the power cord is securely connected at both ends to prevent any damage to the computer or electrical hazards. Step 2: Press the Power Button Locate the power button, usually found on the front or top of the computer case or on the keyboard for laptops. Press the button firmly until the computer starts making sounds and the screen lights up. Guideline: Wait for a few seconds after pressing the power button before releasing it, as some computers require a brief delay. Step 3: Listen for Startup Sounds Pay attention to any sounds your computer makes during startup. These noises can indicate whether there are hardware issues or if everything is functioning properly. For example, a series of short beeps may suggest problems with the RAM modules. Consult your computer's user manual or manufacturer's website for information about specific startup sounds and their meanings. Step 4: Observe the BIOS Screen The Basic Input/Output System (BIOS) screen appears briefly upon booting up and displays essential system information such as CPU type, installed memory, hard drive capacity, and BIOS version. Familiarize yourself with this screen to monitor your computer's basic components and settings. To access more advanced configurations, press the corresponding function key (F2, F10, ESC, etc.) when prompted during startup. However, avoid changing any settings unless you have experience modifying BIOS configurations or under the guidance of an expert. Step 5: Monitor the Boot Process After the BIOS screen disappears, the OS loader begins loading the operating system. During this stage, you might see text-based messages detailing which devices the computer is checking for an OS installation. If multiple drives are present, the computer should prioritize the primary boot device (usually the internal hard drive). You can change the boot order in the BIOS setup menu if needed. Once the OS has loaded successfully, you will reach the login screen or desktop environment. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Keep your computer clean by dusting it regularly to maintain proper airflow and cooling. A clogged computer can lead to overheating, reduced lifespan, and poor performance. * Check for updates frequently. Installing the latest drivers, firmware, and security patches ensures compatibility with new software applications, improved stability, and enhanced protection against cyber threats. * Create regular backups of important files to safeguard them from data loss due to hardware failure, accidental deletion, or malicious attacks. Utilize cloud storage services, external drives, or network attached storage (NAS) solutions for added redundancy and peace of mind. * Perform routine maintenance tasks like disk cleanup, defragmentation, and antivirus scans to keep your computer running smoothly and safely. Scheduling automated tasks can help save time and effort while ensuring consistent care. * Customize your computer's power management options according to your preferences. Adjust sleep modes, display timeout, and hibernation settings to balance energy efficiency and responsiveness based on usage patterns. She would watch it as it rolled far away, and followed it with her eyes until a new adventure began. The shared storytelling palette known as the Marvel Universe was unveiled in 1961, when Goodman responded to the growing interest in superhero books by commissioning writer Stan Lee and artist Jack Kirby to create the Fantastic Four. With the release of "Fantastic Four" no. 1 (November 1961), readers were introduced to a super heroic setting that was, nevertheless, rooted in the real world. Lee and Kirby tried to make their comic book characters more original by allowing them to interact with each other in a realistic fashion, including heroes often fighting or arguing with each other. This trend continued with a flood of other superhero characters introduced by Marvel Comics during the early 1960s, including Spider-Man, the Incredible Hulk, and the X-Men. Lee wrote the majority of Marvel's books during that time, and Jack Kirby and Steve Ditko were the most important and influential artists. Title: How to Become a Telephone Operator Telephone operators play a crucial role in connecting calls and providing assistance to callers. This job requires excellent communication skills, patience, and attention to detail. Here's an in-depth guide on becoming a telephone operator. **Step 1: Understand the Role of a Telephone Operator** A telephone operator is responsible for answering incoming calls, assisting with routing calls, providing information, handling emergency situations, and ensuring customer satisfaction. They may also handle billing issues or service problems. Key Tip: Familiarize yourself with different types of telephone systems such as PBX (Private Branch Exchange), VoIP (Voice over Internet Protocol), and traditional landline switchboards. **Step 2: Meet the Educational Requirements** Most employers require at least a high school diploma or equivalent GED certificate. Some positions might demand postsecondary education like vocational training programs or associate's degrees in fields like communications technology or business administration. Guideline: Completing relevant coursework can enhance your knowledge about telecommunications equipment, computer applications, and customer service techniques which are beneficial for this profession. **Step 3: Develop Necessary Skills** Excellent verbal communication is essential for interacting effectively with customers. Active listening ensures that you understand what the caller needs and allows you to respond appropriately. Patience and empathy help when dealing with frustrated or upset individuals. Also, multitasking abilities come handy while managing multiple lines simultaneously. Key Tips: Practice clear enunciation and develop a friendly tone to establish rapport quickly. Improve typing speed for efficient message taking and note making. **Step 4: Acquire Certifications (Optional)** Although not always necessary, obtaining certifications from organizations like The International Academy of Communication Arts & Sciences adds credibility to your resume. These certificates validate your proficiency in areas like Call Management, Directory Assistance, and Customer Service Techniques. **Step 5: Apply for Jobs** Search online job boards, company websites, and employment agencies for vacancies. Tailor your cover letter and CV highlighting your interpersonal skills, technical expertise, and any previous experience in related roles. Guidelines: Be honest about your qualifications and emphasize your ability to provide top-notch customer service. Highlight instances where you demonstrated problem-solving capabilities or exceeded expectations in past roles. **Step 6: Prepare for Interviews** Research common interview questions asked for telephone operator positions. Brush up on your product/service knowledge if applicable. Anticipate behavioral questions designed to gauge your capacity for calmly addressing challenging scenarios. Key Tips: Demonstrate professionalism during interviews by dressing appropriately, arriving early, maintaining eye contact, and expressing enthusiasm for the position. **Step 7: Complete Training Programs** Once hired, expect comprehensive onboarding covering topics like company policies, safety procedures, operational protocols, and technological competencies. You will likely undergo hands-on training using specific hardware and software systems. Guidelines: Pay close attention during training sessions, take notes, ask clarifying questions, and practice new skills diligently. Remember, being a successful telephone operator involves continuous learning and improvement. Stay updated with advancements in telecom technologies and regularly seek feedback to refine your approach. With dedication and perseverance, you can thrive in this rewarding career! Title: How to Create a Mummy Case Art in Crayon Creating mummy case art using crayons is an engaging and creative way to learn about ancient Egyptian culture while developing your artistic skills. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step with clear instructions and helpful tips. By following these steps, you'll be able to create a beautiful and historically inspired piece of art that can be displayed proudly. ### Materials needed: 1. White paper or cardstock (preferably 8.5 x 11 inches) 2. Brown, gold, black, red, and green crayons 3. Pencil 4. Ruler 5. Eraser 6. Scissors or craft knife (for adult assistance only) 7. Glue stick 8. Colored pencils (optional) **Step 1: Prepare Your Paper** Start by selecting a white sheet of paper or cardstock measuring approximately 8.5 x 11 inches. If necessary, trim the paper down to size. A sturdy surface like cardstock will help prevent wrinkles when applying glue later in this project. **Step 2: Draw Guidelines** Using a ruler and pencil, draw horizontal lines spaced one inch apart across the width of the page, starting from the top edge. These lines will serve as guides for creating the bandages wrapped around the mummy case. Make sure they are evenly spaced and straight. We need to give him some medicine and a nail to help his wing heal." Lily watched as the doctor gave the bird some medicine and put a tiny nail in its wing. When a Federal Reserve Bank receives a cash deposit from a bank, it checks the individual notes to determine whether they are fit for future circulation. About one-third of the notes that the Fed receives are not fit, and the Fed destroys them. As shown in the table below, the life of a note varies according to its denomination. For example, a $1 bill, which gets the greatest use, remains in circulation an average of 5.9 years; a $100 bill lasts about 15 years. Value: Tints and Shades The lightness or darkness of a color is called its value. You can find the values of a color by making its tints and shades. Tints are light values that are made by mixing a color with white. For example, pink is a tint of red, and light blue is a tint of blue. Shades are dark values that are made by mixing a color with black. Maroon is a shade of red, and navy is a shade of blue. This painting by Vincent Van Gogh, Fields in a Rising Storm, has tints and shades of blue in the sky, and tints and shades of green in the fields. TRY IT! Start with some paint in your favorite color. Mix it with different amounts of white to make tints, and different amounts of black to make shades. Then try creating a picture or a design that uses all these tints and shades. Information Please Database, 2007 Pearson Education, Inc. All rights reserved. It has an arrow which points in the direction of the North Magnetic Pole. A compass is very useful for navigating on oceans and in deserts, or other places where there are few landmarks. It appears that careful thought went into both the Phoenician invention and the Greek adaptation, but no details survive of either process. Understanding and Graphing the Inverse Function - 0:06 Inverse Functions - 2:24 Five Steps to Inversing a Function - 2:46 Examples - 5:31 Graphing Inverse Functions - 6:52 Lesson Summary Did You Know This lesson is part of a free course that leads to real college credit accepted by 2,900 colleges. If you use a function to map a to b, is there a way to go back from b to a again? Learn how to find and graph inverse functions so that you can turn a into b and back into a. If I tell you that I have a function that maps the number of feet in some distance to the number of inches in that distance, you might tell me that the function is y=f(x) where the input x is the number of feet and the output y is the number of inches. You might even tell me that y = f(x) = 12x, because there are 12 inches in every foot. But what if I told you that I wanted a function that does the exact opposite? What if I want a function to take the number of inches as input and return the number of feet as output? Could you tell me what this function is? Inverse functions are exactly that. If we have a function y=f(x), then the inverse function is written as y= f^(-1)(x), and it does the exact opposite of the function. What happens if you put a function and its inverse into a composite function such as f^(-1)(f(x))? Let's take a look at an example. Say we start with 4 feet. Well, our function is f(x)=12x because there are 12 inches in every foot. If we plug in 4 feet to start, then f(4) = 12 * 4 = 48 inches. Now if we take the inverse function, and the inverse function is going to be f^(-1)(x) = x(1/12). So, if we take 48 inches, then our inverse function, f^(-1)(48) = 48 / 12 = 4 feet. Okay, so you might be able to find f(x) and f^(-1)(x) just based on your understanding of inches and feet, but how do you do it in general? Five Steps to Inversing a Function - Write your function out in terms of x and y: y=f(x). - Swap the x and y variables: x=f(y). - Solve for y as a function of x. - Set y = f^(-1)(x). - Check the composite function: f^(-1)(f(x)). Following these steps, let's say we have a function f(x) = 3(x - 1) + 2. We're going to write this out in terms of x and y: y = 3(x - 1) + 2. Then we're going swap the x and y variables, so we're going to write this as x = 3(y - 1) + 2. This can be a confusing step if you're not careful, but at its heart, all you're doing is putting x everywhere you see y and putting y everywhere you see x. Then you're going to solve for y as a function of x. So I'm going to subtract 2 from both sides, x - 2 = 3(y - 1), divide both sides by 3, (x - 2) / 3 = y - 1 and add 1 to both sides and I end up with y = 1 + (x - 2)/3. I'm going to call what's on the right-hand side my inverse function, f^(-1)(x) = 1 + (x-2)/3. Finally, I'm going to check my answer, so I'm going to find f^(-1) of (f(x)). To do this, I'm going to write f(x) = 3(x-1) + 2. I'm going to plug that in as input for my inverse function, so f^(-1)(x) = 1 + ((3(x-1) + 2) - 2)/3. I have my input here, so I'm just going to solve and simplify for f^(-1)(x) = 1 + (3(x-1))/3: f^(-1)(x) = 1 + x - 1. And sure enough, f^(-1)(f(x)) = x, which is exactly what we'd expect. So what about a function like y = round(x)? Remember that round(x) just rounds our input to the nearest integer: round(4.2) = 4. However, round(4.8) = 5 and round(5.1) = 5. In this case, do you think that you can find an inverse function that can take 5 and give your either 5.1 or 4.8? No, round(x) is a function that has no inverse. Graphing Inverse Functions What about the function f(x) = x^3 + 3x? I can write it out in terms of x and y: y = x^3 + 3x. I can then swap the variables, x = y^3 + 3y. I can then solve it for y - but that's not immediately obvious to me. Is there another way? Let's go back and look at an easier function, like f(x) = 3x - 6. I end up with a graph that looks like this, a simple line. Now I'm going to graph the inverse, which is f^(-1)(x) = (x + 6)/3. So the inverse is this blue line; it looks a lot like the original function, except it's mirrored. The inverse function will undo the function. That means that the inverse function of the function will give you back what you started with. But not all functions will have inverses. For example, y= round(x) doesn't have an inverse. You can find the inverse function with our five-step process. If you graph a function and its inverse, they're 45-degree reflections of one another. That's an easy way to find the inverse or get an idea of what the inverse function looks like for really complex functions. Ask Our Experts Response times may vary by topic. There is no one scientific method. Some fields of science are based on mathematical models, such as physics and climate science. Other fields, such as many fields of social science, have rough theories and rely more on patterns that emerge from their data. Sometimes scientists focus on testing and confirming hypotheses, but open-ended exploration is also important. Some scientific fields use laboratory experiments. Others collect observations from real-world situations. Many areas of science are quantitative, emphasizing numerical data and mathematical analysis. But some areas, especially in social science, use qualitative methods, such as interviews or detailed observations of human or animal behavior. Focusing too much one kind of method can lead us to ignore knowledge produced using other methods. What are you going to do with that hm? Know your phonemes! No proof so far links this to an exact prenatal process. Tests give the idea that this may be from the immune system. This is a immune reaction from the mother against a substance important to the development of the male fetus. This reaction is even more probable after every successful male birth. It is thought that this immune effect changes future males prenatal development. This process is the maternal immunization hypothesis (MIH). This starts when cells from a male fetus enter the circulation of the mother. The immune system of the mother cannot support the Y proteins because she is female. This causes the development of antibodies. An antibody travels through the placenta into the section with the fetus. From here, an antibody crosses the blood-brain barrier of the brain of the fetus. This alters sex differences in brain structures relative to sexual orientation. This cause the son to experience more attraction to a man than a woman. See we'll find the ones that stick to it. Most vertebrates (animals with backbones) have tails; many invertebrates (animals without backbones), such as spiders and certain insects, have no tails. All vertebrates have tails during at least one stage of their life histories. The frog, for example, has a tail as a tadpole, and the human embryo has a tail during its fifth to eighth week. A few human beings are born with small, imperfectly developed tails, which can be removed by surgery. Tails vary widely as to size, shape, and use. The cow's tail is flexible and has a tuft of hair at the end, making it useful in whisking away flies and other insects. American monkeys and Old World chameleons use their long, slender tails for grasping. The tails of animals that live in water, such as fish and whales, have strong muscles and serve as aids to moving and steering. Birds also use their tails for steering, in flight. The male pheasant makes a colorful display of its tail feathers to attract the female. In some kinds of lizards, the tail breaks off easily, often allowing the animal to escape from the grasp of a predator. A new tail is then grown. But she didn't really wasn't really into it. Title: How to Delete a WhatsApp Message You've Already Sent WhatsApp is one of the most popular messaging apps worldwide, with over 2 billion users. One feature that sets it apart from other messaging platforms is the ability to delete sent messages within a certain time frame. This guide will walk you through the process of deleting a message you have already sent on WhatsApp, providing in-depth explanations for each step and offering key tips along the way. ### Part 1: Check Eligibility Criteria Before proceeding, ensure your device meets these requirements: 1. The recipient(s) must be using the latest version of WhatsApp. If they haven't updated their app yet, they won't see the 'This message was deleted' notification when you remove your message. 2. Both you and the recipient(s) must have an active internet connection at the time of deletion. Messages can only be deleted while connected to the internet. 3. You need to act quickly since there is a limited window during which you can delete a message after sending it (currently up to 1 hour, 8 minutes, and 16 seconds). ### Part 2: Delete the Message Follow these steps to delete the sent message: 1. Open WhatsApp on your phone or tablet. Make sure you are logged into your account. 2. Navigate to the chat where you sent the message. It could be an individual conversation or a group chat. 3. Find and locate the specific message you wish to delete. Remember, you cannot edit a message; you can only choose between two options - either delete the message for everyone or just for yourself. 4. Long press on the selected message until a menu appears at the top of the screen. On Android devices, tap the trash bin icon next to the message, then select 'Delete for Everyone.' For iOS devices, tap the 'Delete' option followed by 'Delete for Everyone.' 5. Confirm the action when prompted. A confirmation message will appear indicating whether the deletion was successful or if it failed due to various reasons like delayed delivery or lack of connectivity. Key Tips: * To avoid confusion, try to provide context around the deleted message so recipients know what information has been removed. * Keep in mind that even though the message disappears, its content might still be visible via notifications alerts on some devices. ### Part 3: Understanding What Happens After Deletion After successfully deleting a message, this is what happens: * Recipient(s): They will receive a notice saying 'This message was deleted,' instead of seeing the actual text. However, depending on their settings and device capabilities, they may have seen the message before deletion. * Your copy: Once deleted, the message vanishes from both your phone and the recipient's device. But keep in mind that backups made prior to deletion might contain the original message. Guidelines: * Always double-check your messages before hitting send, as once a message leaves your device, it becomes vulnerable to being read by others. * Consider setting up two-step verification for added security on your WhatsApp account. By following these steps carefully, you can effectively delete a WhatsApp message you've already sent within the allowed timeframe. Just remember to check eligibility criteria first and understand the implications post-deletion. [13 seconds while Chi starts to get the tea set out] So what'd mommy do with the chalk? I bet they need an enormous tub. ah, your penny's not in the fire. Welcome, Grade School Students! Today, we're going to learn about something called "Null Space." Now, don't let the fancy name scare you - it's just a way of talking about solving equations. And who doesn't like solving puzzles? Imagine you have a bunch of numbers, arranged in a grid, like this: ``` 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 ``` This is called a matrix. It's just a fancy word for a grid of numbers. Now, imagine you want to find out if there's another grid of numbers that you can multiply by this matrix, and get the answer 0. This is where the Null Space comes in. The Null Space is the set of all possible grids of numbers that you can multiply by our original matrix, and get the answer 0. Let's call our original matrix 'A'. So, the Null Space is written as N(A). To find the Null Space, first, you need to solve the equation Ax = 0. Here, x stands for a grid of numbers - just like our original matrix. Then, write down the solutions in what's called "parametric vector form". The vectors you see here are the basis of N(A), or the Null Space. Now, why do we care about the Null Space? Well, it has some special properties that make it useful when we're working with matrices. First, the Zero Vector (or a grid filled with zeros) is always in the Null Space. Second, if you add two vectors from the Null Space together, you still get a vector that's in the Null Space. Lastly, if you take any vector from the Null Space and multiply it by a number, you still get a vector that's in the Null Space. These properties are pretty cool because they tell us that the Null Space is a special kind of mathematical structure called a "subspace". So next time you come across a puzzle involving a grid of numbers, remember the Null Space. It could help you solve the puzzle faster than ever before! Happy learning! Which of the following cubes can be made from these nets? Before you read much further I think it will help if you get yourself three cubes that are the same size. Maybe if you are at home they could be the kind that very young toddlers have to play with, made from wood or plastic. If you are at school it should be no problem, but avoid those cubes that have bits for connecting together. Just three cubes of the same size. Place just one of these cubes down in front of you on the table. I'm going to ask a question that gets repeated each time we try a new idea. Here's the question: "If you are allowed to walk around the table, bend down low, move your head around in any way that you like, get as near to the cubes as you like, etc. then how many square faces could you see altogether, without lifting up or touching the cubes in any way?'' Well I guess that with one cube it is quite easy, yes it's 5. Now use two cubes and put them in any way that you like. Well, here are three arrangements I did earlier. The first one lets me see 9, the second one 8 and with the two on their own it's 10! So the answer to the question depends on how the cubes are arranged. I suppose that's pretty obvious. Well, what about three cubes? Here are some arrangements - just some. The first has 11; the second has 13; the 3 on their own have 15; the last one has 14 and two-thirds. Now that's a strange one - you can see why we need "non-connecting'' cubes. "How did I get 14 and two-thirds?'' you may be asking. It's the sort of question I ask the children I'm working with. I'll tell you. The red shows 5 whole faces, the green and the blue each show 4 whole faces. There is about 1/3 of the underneath of the red I could see if I creep down low and look up when I'm very close. There is 2/3 on top of both the blue and the green that are visible. That makes a total of 14 and 2/3. So you take your 3 cubes and see what your answer to the question is each time you make a new arrangement. It's probably good to record your answers. This is very easy with some art packages on the computer. You might like to get an arrangement that produces a particular number of faces visible. See what your largest/smallest answer is. When you think you have got sufficient answers, probably something in the region of 10, then use a fourth cube and see how you get on now. Good Luck. Today is two seventeen two thousand ten. "Aaaaaaa," Cries the Baby A Beginning Reading Lesson By Janie Colvin Rationale: This lesson teaches children about the /a/ correspondence, and how to read /a/ by associating it with a crying baby. Students will learn to recognize /a/ in spoken words by learning a meaningful representation (a baby crying) and the letter symbol A. Students will spell and read words containing /a/ in a Letterbox lesson, along with reading a decodable book that focuses on the /a/ sound. Materials: Graphic image of a crying baby, cover-up critter, letter boxes, primary paper, letter tiles (a,t,h,m,p,d,r,g,c,l,s,s,f,b,n), poster with tongue twister written on it ("Andrew and Alice asked if Annie's animals were agitated."), white board, overhead/document cam, worksheet (listed at the bottom of the page), books for each student (A Cat Nap), and a list of spelling words on a poster or whiteboard for the students to read (lap, mat, Sam, gas, bank, glass, crab, splat) 1. Say: "In order to become expert readers we need to learn the code that tells us how to pronounce words. All letters make different sounds as we move our mouths a certain way. Today class, we are going to learn about the /a/ sound. When I say /a/, I think of a baby crying, "Ahhhhhhh." (Show graphic image). 2. Say: "The letter we are going to learn about today is a." (Have students take out primary paper and pencil). "Let's practice writing a on our primary paper. (Model on the board how to write the letter a). "Start at the fence line and make a curved line down until you touch the sidewalk, but don't stop here. Continue the curve around until you end up where you started. Then draw a straight line back down, and stop on the sidewalk." (As students practice drawing a row of a's, walk around the room observing and checking if they are correctly writing the letter a). 3. Say: "Before we learn about the spelling of /a/, we need to listen for it in some words. When I say /a/ in words, my mouth opens and lengthens, and my mouth looks like a baby's mouth when he/she cries. (Make vocal gesture for /a/.) I'll show you first: class. I heard /a/ and I felt my mouth open and lengthen (make a circle motion around lips). Now I'm going to see if I hear /a/ in school. Hmm, I didn't hear /a/ in school, and my mouth didn't open and lengthen. "That's not it." Do you hear /a/ in head, made, snow, rain, coat, bag, stamp, or lips?" (Have students make a circle motion around their lips when they feel their mouths make the /a/ movement). 4. Say: "Now I am going to teach each of you a tongue tickler that will help you remember the sound that /a/ makes." "Andrew and Alice asked if Annie's animals were agitated." (I will briefly review what the word agitated means before we say the tongue tickler together). "Let's say it together! Now let's say it again, and if you hear the /a/ sound in a word, I want you to raise your hand. (Repeat tongue twister). Now I want each of you to stretch out the /a/ sound. (Ex: Aaaaaandrew aaaand Aaaaalice ) Good Job!" 5. (Have students take out their letterboxes and letters). Say: "We are going to use what we just learned about the letter a to spell words. I will call out a word and you can spell it using the letterboxes. Before each word I call out I will tell you how many boxes to use. Each sound or mouth move in the word will go in a box. For example, the word I am going to spell is hat. I will use three boxes (draw three boxes on the board), because it has three sounds. The first sound I hear is /h/. I will place the letter h in the first box (model on board). Now it might help to say the word again to yourself, hat. The second sound I hear is /a/. We just learned the letter a stands for /a/, so I will place the a in the second box (model on the board). The last sound I hear is /t/. I will place the t in the third box (model on board). I spelled the word hat. Now you try." The words I will call out are: Lap, mat, Sam, gas (3) bank, glass, crab (4), and splat (5). After the students spell a word, the class will spell the word and I will write it on the board. After writing the words on the board, the class will read each word together. 6. Now, I will divide the students into partners. I will give each partner a copy of the book, A Cat Nap. I will ask each partner to go back and forth reading a page to each other. (Remind students if they are reading and get stuck, that there are things they can do to help themselves). Say: "First, try to read the word by covering parts of it up like I demonstrated for you earlier. Then read the sentence all the way through. Think about if the sentence makes sense. Then change words that do not make sense. After you are finished correcting, always make sure you reread the sentence one time through with the corrections that you made. I will be walking around to help you if you need it." 7. To end this lesson, I will read the story to the students and we will discuss and talk about the story as we read. The students will reflect on the story. For the next lesson we will use this book by rereading a familiar text. 8. To assess the students they will each be given a worksheet where they will be asked to circle the picture of the objects that contain the a=/a/ sound. The students must say the name of each picture aloud, and then circle the pictures in which they hear /a/. "Aaaaaaaaa!" The baby cried. By: Ashley Farrow Aaaaaaa!! The Crying Baby. These chief specimens of the marriage provision were supported on the right by a couple of kitchen dressers, each fitted complete with their cups, dishes, and plates, in their turn followed by two dumb-waiters, two family Bibles, two warming-pans, and two intermixed sets of chairs. But the position last reached-the chimney-corner-was, after all, the most attractive side of the parallelogram. It was large enough to admit, in addition to Geoffrey himself, Geoffrey's wife, her chair, and her work-table, entirely within the line of the mantel, without danger or even inconvenience from the heat of the fire; and was spacious enough overhead to allow of the insertion of wood poles for the hanging of bacon, which were cloaked with long shreds of soot, floating on the draught like the tattered banners on the walls of ancient aisles. These points were common to most chimney corners of the neighbourhood; but one feature there was which made Geoffrey's fireside not only an object of interest to casual aristocratic visitors-to whom every cottage fireside was more or less a curiosity-but the admiration of friends who were accustomed to fireplaces of the ordinary hamlet model. Roman carpenters and shipwrights at once set to work, studying how it was put together, and thinking out devices by which it could be improved. While the shipwrights were busy the men practised rowing on dry land. The most famous improvement invented by the Romans was the 'crow'. Fighting with siblings is an inevitable part of growing up in many families. While these disputes can be frustrating, they also provide opportunities for learning important life skills such as conflict resolution, empathy, and communication. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to handle fights with siblings: **Step 1: Stay Calm** When a disagreement arises, take a deep breath and try to stay calm. This will help you think clearly and rationally, which is essential for resolving conflicts peacefully. If you feel yourself getting angry or upset, remove yourself from the situation until you are able to regain your composure. *Key tip:* Remember that yelling, name calling, or physical violence only escalates the situation and makes it harder to find a solution. **Step 2: Identify the Issue** Try to determine what started the argument. Was it a misunderstanding? A competition over resources (toys, attention, etc.)? Or perhaps one sibling feels jealous or left out? Understanding the root cause of the dispute can help you address it more effectively. *Guideline:* Encourage open communication between siblings by asking them to explain their perspectives without interruption. **Step 3: Establish Ground Rules** Before attempting to resolve the issue, set some ground rules for the conversation. These might include taking turns speaking, listening actively, respecting each other's opinions, and avoiding blame or criticism. *Key tip:* Using "I" statements instead of "you" statements can help prevent defensiveness and promote understanding. For example, say "I felt hurt when..." rather than "You always..." **Step 4: Brainstorm Solutions Together** Once everyone has had a chance to share their feelings and perspective, work together to come up with potential solutions. Encourage creativity and cooperation - sometimes the best answers come from combining ideas. *Guideline:* Make sure all parties involved agree on the chosen solution; otherwise, resentment may build and lead to future conflicts. **Step 5: Implement the Solution** Put the agreed-upon solution into action. Monitor progress closely to ensure both sides are following through on their commitments. *Key tip:* Be prepared to make adjustments if the initial plan isn't working as well as expected. Flexibility is crucial in finding lasting solutions. **Step 6: Reflect on the Experience** After the issue has been resolved, take time to reflect on what happened and discuss any lessons learned. This can help siblings develop empathy and improve their conflict resolution skills. *Guideline:* Praise effort and improvement, rather than just focusing on the outcome. Positive reinforcement encourages continued growth and development. By following these steps, you'll be better equipped to navigate fights with siblings and turn potentially negative experiences into valuable learning opportunities. Remember that conflict is a natural part of relationships, but handling it constructively can strengthen bonds and foster personal growth. Jason'has been playing with him all day. It will print text to the screen, and then exit. put "Hello, world!" Pretty simple, eh? As you probably deduced, put prints text. For now, we'll be using it to output to the screen. Next, we'll learn how to gather input from the user using get. We'll also declare two variables, name and age. The program will get the user's name and age, and repeat it back to them. var name : string %A string is text var age : nat %Natural number put "What is your name?" get name put "" put "How old are you?" get age put "Hello, ", name, " you are ", age, " years old!" First, we have to declare two variables. Turing lets you declare variables anywhere in the program, but it's usually a good idea to declare them at the top. Now why was age a natural number? Why not an integer? Well, in Turing, natural numbers are all positive integers, including zero (0,1,2,3...). Integers, however, include negative numbers as well as positive numbers. The user's age shouldn't be a negative number, so it doesn't need to be an integer. Second, did you notice the % followed by some text? Those are called comments. They start at the percent-sign and continue until the end of the line. They are meant to be messages to you, the programmer, about your program. Turing doesn't care about them. They have no meaning except to the person reading the code, of which they may well be of great importance. It is important that you use meaningful comments. When you come back eight weeks later, or someone else reads your code, you may have no idea what that code you wrote meant. After we have two variables set up, we proceed to ask the user their name...they have to know what to enter! Then we say get name. This means, "Let the user type in a string, and store it under the variable name for later use. Same for age. Finally, we regurgitate the information back to the user. Make sure you put spaces before closing the quotation marks, or else there won't be any spaces before and after their name and age! One last thing: Turing uses token-oriented input. This means that when the user inputs a space, it is treated the same as a line break. But we don't want this in our program, because what if the user enters their first and last name? Therefore, we tell turing "only get the next variable when they press enter." [as Sally knocks ball off of the tables and the ball hits him in the face] Hello young mathematicians! Today, we're going to learn about a fun and interesting concept in mathematics called sequences of functions. You might already be familiar with sequences in numbers, like 1, 2, 3, 4, ... or 1/2, 2/3, 3/4, 4/5, ... But did you know that we can also have sequences made up of whole functions? That means each term in our sequence is a different rule that tells us how to turn a number into another number. Let me give you an example using pictures. Imagine we have a bunch of shapes that are all squished down into skinny rectangles, like this: As we go from one rectangle to the next, we notice something special - each rectangle is just a little bit taller than the last one, but they all have the same width. We could say that these rectangles form a sequence of functions, where each function takes a number between 0 and 1 and gives us back the height of the corresponding rectangle. Now, here comes the cool part. Even though each individual rectangle has a finite area, when we keep adding more and more rectangles together, their combined area grows and grows without any upper limit! This idea is similar to the mathematical concept of "a sequence of continuous functions that converges to the zero function and whose integrals increase without bound." In plain English, it means that even though the heights of our rectangles get closer and closer to 0, the total area under those tiny heights keeps getting bigger and bigger. To visualize this, imagine stacking all of our skinnier and taller rectangles on top of each other, forming a single shape. As we add more rectangles, this shape gets larger and larger in area, extending upward without ever reaching a maximum size. Finally, let's explore the bonus question mentioned in the original text. Can you create a sequence of functions where the individual rules (functions) become more and more complicated until they no longer follow a clear pattern, making them impossible to integrate like a normal function? The answer is yes! One famous example involves creating a function that is equal to 1 at every rational number (numbers that can be expressed as fractions), and 0 everywhere else. Since there are infinitely many rational numbers packed tightly within any interval, trying to calculate the area underneath this wacky function would involve dealing with an infinite mess of ones and zeros. I height get you something to chew on. What does she look like when she puts this on? Title: Distributing Balls into Bags: A Fun Math Problem Hello young mathematicians! Today, let's explore a fun problem involving distributing balls into bags while learning some new concepts along the way. This will not only challenge your math skills but also teach you some handy techniques for solving similar problems in the future. Imagine having $m$ identical balls that you want to place inside $n$ different bags. There's one rule though - every bag needs to have at least $k$ balls in it. The question asks us to find out in how many different ways we could distribute these balls according to this rule. Don't worry; we won't jump straight into complicated formulas or big numbers. Instead, let's break down the problem step by step using tools familiar to you. Step 1: Place "at least $k$" balls in each bag To start with, since each of the $n$ bags should have at least $k$ balls, go ahead and add exactly $k$ balls to each bag first. Now, you may think that we just used up $kn$ balls, but hold on tight! These preliminary distributed balls still count towards our final arrangement because they meet the requirement set forth in the problem. Step 2: Calculate the remaining balls Since we initially added $kn$ balls to fulfill the minimum requirement per bag, we know that we still need to account for $(m - kn)$ balls left unplaced. It's time to bring those sneaky extra balls into play. Step 3: Arrange the remaining balls without constraints Now comes the exciting part! We can employ the ever-popular ball & stick approach to arrange the leftover $(m - kn)$ balls among the $n$ bags. Remember playing with colored blocks or toy bricks? That's precisely the idea behind this technique! To make things easier, picture placing each additional ball like adding another block onto its respective bag-pile. Let's call the number of additional balls assigned to Bag #1 as $x_1$; similarly, assign $x_2$ to Bag #2, all the way up till $x_n$ for Bag #n. Our objective then becomes finding the number of possible combinations of $x_1, x_2, ... , x_n$ which satisfy the following conditions: i) Each variable ($x_1, x_2, ... , x_n$) represents the number of extra balls in their corresponding bag. ii) All the variables together should sum up to the remaining balls we calculated earlier, i.e., $x_1 + x_2 + ... + x_n = (m - kn)$. iii) Any value greater than or equal to zero works for each variable, meaning $x_1, x_2, ... , x_n >= 0$. With practice, you'll learn more strategies to tackle such questions confidently. But don't forget to enjoy yourself while exploring mathematical curiosities like today's ball distribution conundrum! Happy counting! Famous architects include: Frank Lloyd Wright, Fazlur Khan, Bruce Graham, Edward Durell Stone, Daniel Burnham, Adrian Smith, Frank Gehry, Gottfried B hm, I. M. Pei, Antoni Gaud , and Oscar Niemeyer. It's funny like as we've done this the first time she was really attracted to color. Title: How to Hide a Folder on Windows **Introduction:** Hidden folders can be useful when you want to keep certain files or directories private, away from prying eyes. This tutorial will guide you through the process of hiding a folder on your Windows computer using built-in features. Note that this method does not provide absolute security as advanced users may still access hidden folders via specific settings modifications or third-party software. **Step 1: Open File Explorer** Press `Windows Key + E` simultaneously to launch File Explorer, an essential tool for managing files and folders within the Windows operating system. **Step 2: Navigate to the Desired Folder** Using the navigation panel on the left side of the window, browse to locate the folder you wish to hide. If it's located inside another folder, navigate into that one first until you reach your target. **Step 3: Change Properties of the Folder** Right-click on the chosen folder, then select 'Properties' from the context menu that appears. A new dialog box titled "[Folder Name] Properties" should now appear. **Step 4: Access the Customize Tab** In the property windows, click on the tab labeled 'Customize'. Here, you'll find options related to customizing the appearance and behavior of your selected folder. **Step 5: Check the 'Hidden' Option** Under the 'Options available in this view' section, check the box next to 'Hidden'. By doing so, you are instructing Windows to categorize this folder as hidden, effectively concealing it from regular view. **Step 6: Confirm Changes** Click 'Apply', followed by 'OK' at the bottom right corner of the properties window. The changes take effect immediately; however, depending on your current viewing settings, the folder might still be visible. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * After hiding a folder, if it remains visible, ensure that you have unchecked 'Show hidden files, folders, and drives' under 'View' > 'Options' > 'Change folder and search options' in File Explorer. * Remember, anyone with administrative rights could potentially reverse these steps and reveal hidden folders. Therefore, don't rely solely on this technique for sensitive data protection. Instead, consider encryption or more robust solutions designed for privacy concerns. * When sharing your computer, remember to recheck 'Show hidden files, folders, and drives' beforehand if you need to access any previously hidden items. Alternatively, type '%appdata%' (without quotes) in the address bar of File Explorer to directly open the Roaming folder, which is typically hidden but always accessible to the user who created it. By following these clear and easy-to-understand steps, you can successfully hide a folder on your Windows machine, enhancing your privacy and control over your personal information. Internet Explorer has been subjected to many security vulnerabilities and concerns: Much of the spyware, adware, and computer viruses across the Internet are made possible by exploitable bugs and flaws in the security architecture of Internet Explorer, sometimes requiring nothing more than viewing of a malicious web page in order to install themselves. This is known as a "drive-by install". There are also attempts to trick the user into installing malicious software by misrepresenting the software's true purpose in the description section of an ActiveX security alert. Title: How to Do a Dramatic Pause A dramatic pause is a technique used in public speaking, acting, storytelling, or any form of oral communication to create suspense, emphasize an important point, or allow time for emotion to sink in. It can be a powerful tool when used correctly, making your words more impactful and engaging. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do a dramatic pause effectively. **Step 1: Understand the Purpose of a Dramatic Pause** The first step towards mastering the dramatic pause is understanding its purpose. A well-timed pause adds weight to what you are saying, giving your audience time to absorb the information and prepare for the next statement. This heightened anticipation can make your speech more compelling and memorable. **Key Tip:** Use pauses sparingly. Overuse can diminish their effectiveness and turn them into filler sounds rather than strategic tools. **Step 2: Identify Key Points in Your Speech** Determine where a dramatic pause would add value. These moments often include significant revelations, emotional peaks, transitions between ideas, or buildups toward climactic statements. Mark these spots as potential 'pause points'. **Guideline:** As a rule of thumb, aim for one pause every minute or two depending on the length of your presentation. **Step 3: Practice Timing** Timing is crucial for a successful dramatic pause. Too short, and it may go unnoticed; too long, and you could lose your audience's attention. The ideal duration varies based on context but generally falls within 2-5 seconds. **Key Tip:** Start by practicing with predetermined timings (e.g., count silently to three) until you become comfortable adjusting the length dynamically according to the situation. **Step 4: Maintain Eye Contact During the Pause** Maintaining eye contact during a pause keeps your audience engaged. Avoid looking away or down, which might suggest uncertainty or discomfort. Instead, hold your gaze steady, using body language to underscore the importance of this moment. **Guideline:** If maintaining direct eye contact feels uncomfortable, try focusing on a general area of the room or at different individuals throughout the pause. **Step 5: Control Your Breathing** Proper breathing control ensures that your voice doesn't waver during the pause. Take slow, deep breaths before and after the pause to maintain vocal stability. **Key Tip:** Try inhaling through your nose and exhaling through your mouth while counting slowly to five. Repeat several times until relaxed and focused. **Step 6: Modulate Your Tone Before and After the Pause** Changing your tone just before and immediately after a pause highlights its significance. Lowering your volume slightly beforehand creates intrigue, whereas raising it afterwards delivers emphasis. **Guideline:** Experiment with various inflections to find what works best for your specific content and delivery style. **Step 7: Record Yourself Practicing** Listening back to recordings allows you to evaluate your performance objectively. Pay particular attention to whether the pauses feel natural and appropriately placed. **Key Tip:** Consider filming yourself as well to review nonverbal cues such as facial expressions and gestures. Remember, practice makes perfect. With patience and persistence, incorporating dramatic pauses into your speeches will enhance their overall impact, transforming ordinary presentations into captivating performances. Title: How to Eat Brie Cheese Like a Pro Brie cheese is a type of soft cheese that originated from France. It has a creamy texture and mildly tangy flavor which makes it perfect as an appetizer or dessert cheese. If you're new to eating brie or just want to level up your brie-eating game, this step-by-step guide will help you enjoy every bite like a pro. **Step 1: Choose Your Brie** Before anything else, select the right kind of brie. There are various types available such as classic French Brie de Meaux, Brie de Melun, goat's milk brie, etc., so choose one based on your preference. Make sure to check its expiration date; fresh brie tastes better than old ones. *Key Tip:* Look for bries with white mold rind without any dark spots or cracks indicating spoilage. The interior should be pale yellow, smooth, and unblemished. **Step 2: Let It Reach Room Temperature** Take out your brie from the refrigerator about an hour before serving. Allowing it to warm slightly improves its taste because cold temperatures can numb your palate and make it harder to appreciate the full range of flavors. *Guideline:* Never microwave brie to speed up the process. This alters the delicate balance between fat and protein causing separation resulting in greasy cheese. **Step 3: Cutting & Serving** Cut off a small piece at the tip (the narrower end) of the wheel using a sharp knife. Then slice horizontally towards the center creating a 'V' cut. Remove this wedge carefully. Now you have two halves ready to serve! *Key Tip:* Always use separate utensils for cutting and serving to prevent cross-contamination of different parts of the brie. **Step 4: Pairings** Enhance the flavor by pairing your brie with complementary foods. Fruits like apples, pears, grapes, and dried fruits work well. Nuts, especially walnuts, pecans, or hazelnuts add a nice crunch contrast. You could also try honey, jams, chutneys, or mustards for added sweetness or spice. For bread, consider baguette slices, crackers, or toasted whole grain bread. *Guideline:* Avoid strongly flavored accompaniments that might overpower the subtle nuances of the brie. **Step 5: Tasting Technique** Pick up a piece of brie using your fingers, a fork, or bread. Observe its aroma - does it smell earthy? Milky? Put it in your mouth and let it sit there for a moment to allow the warmth of your tongue to release more flavors. Gently chew once or twice then swallow. Savor the aftertaste left behind. *Key Tip:* Don't rush through tasting. Take your time to fully experience the complexity of brie's flavors. **Step 6: Cleanse Your Palate** Between bites, cleanse your palate with water, plain crackers, or sips of wine. This ensures you get a clear sense of each distinct flavor profile. *Guideline:* Save red wines for stronger cheeses. White wines, champagne, or light beers pair best with brie. Remember, there are no hard rules when enjoying food. Feel free to experiment and discover what works best for you. Happy eating! Funk Up Your Apple Device: A Comprehensive Tutorial ================================================== Introduction ------------ In this extensive guide, we will explore various ways to customize your Apple device, infusing it with funk and personality that truly represents you. Whether you own an iPhone, iPad, or Mac, these steps can help you create a unique user experience tailored to your preferences. We'll discuss customizing settings, installing third-party applications, modifying system appearances, and more. By following our clear, concise, and easy-to-understand instructions, you'll be well on your way to transforming your Apple gadget into something extraordinary. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Remember to back up any data before making significant changes to your device; * Be cautious when granting permissions to third-party apps and ensure they are from trusted sources; * Keep your operating systems updated to avoid compatibility issues and potential security risks; * Enjoy the process! So he had ta go find some new string and put it through. Spring Extension Experiment Hey kids! Today we're going to do a fun science experiment using a spring, some weights, and gravity. This will help us understand how energy works and how it affects things around us. First, let's talk about what a spring is. A spring is a flexible object that can be stretched or compressed. You might have seen a toy called a slinky before - it's a type of spring! Now, imagine we have a spring with a certain stiffness, which we call "k." We also have two weights, "m" and "M," and we want to figure out how far the spring will stretch when the two weights come together after falling from different heights. There are two ways we can figure this out: by solving a math problem and by using something called "conservation of energy." Let's try both methods and see if we get the same answer! Method 1: Math Problem We start by writing down a formula based on Newton's second law of motion, which says that force equals mass times acceleration. In our case, the force comes from the spring pushing back when it gets stretched, so the formula looks like this: (M+m) * d x / dt + k*x = (M+m)*g Here, x represents the position of the weight attached to the spring, g is the acceleration due to gravity, and t stands for time. To simplify this equation, we assume that the combined weight of m and M moves together once they collide. By solving this equation with some specific starting conditions, we can find the maximum distance the spring stretches. Method 2: Conservation of Energy Another way to approach this problem is by thinking about energy conservation. When we drop the heavier weight "M" from a height "h," it gains potential energy equal to M*g*h. Once it hits the lighter weight "m," all that energy turns into movement energy, or kinetic energy. At the moment of impact, these two energies combine. When the weights join forces and continue moving upward together, their total energy remains the same because no energy was lost during the collision. As they rise, however, their potential energy increases while their kinetic energy decreases until they reach zero at the highest point. By setting up an equation representing the conservation of energy at every stage, we can determine the maximum distance the spring extends. Cool, right? Even though these two methods involve more complex ideas like calculus and physics concepts beyond your current schooling, understanding the basics behind them gives you insight into real-life phenomena. So next time you play with a slinky or bounce a ball, remember that there's a lot happening behind the scenes involving energy transfer and conservation! Creating an ISO DVD with Windows 7 is a straightforward process that can be accomplished by following these steps: 1. **Obtain a copy of Windows 7 installation files**: Before you begin, make sure you have access to the original Windows 7 installation media or a valid digital download from a reputable source. This will ensure that your ISO file contains all the necessary drivers, updates, and features required for a successful installation. 2. **Download and Install a Disc Image Burner Software**: To create an ISO image from your Windows 7 installation disc, you'll need specialized software capable of handling this task. There are many free options available such as ImgBurn, CDBurnerXP, or PowerISO. Download one of these programs and install it onto your computer. *Tip*: Always choose reliable sources when downloading any type of software. Avoid clicking on suspicious links or ads that promise quick fixes or free versions of paid products. These could potentially harm your system by introducing malware or viruses. 3. **Insert the Original Windows 7 Disc into Your Computer**: Once you've installed your chosen disc burning software, insert the original Windows 7 DVD into your optical drive. 4. **Select 'Create Image from Disc' Option**: Open your newly installed disc burner program and look for an option labeled something like 'Create Image', 'Make ISO', or 'Read Disc'. Click on this button to start the imaging process. 5. **Choose Output Location**: You'll then be prompted to select where you want to save the resulting ISO file. Choose a location with sufficient storage space (a few gigabytes at least) and remember this path for future reference. 6. **Wait for the Process to Complete**: Depending on the speed of your computer and the size of the disc, creating the ISO image may take several minutes. During this time, avoid using other resource-intensive applications so as not to slow down the progress. 7. **Finalize the ISO File**: After the imaging process has completed, finalize the ISO file if given the option. Finalizing ensures data integrity and prevents further modifications to the file. 8. **Test the ISO File**: It's good practice to test the new ISO file before proceeding with writing it onto a blank DVD. Mount the ISO using virtual drive software like Virtual CloneDrive or built-in tools found in newer operating systems. Run through the setup procedure within the mounted environment to confirm everything works correctly. 9. **Prepare a Blank DVD**: If the ISO passes testing, prepare a blank writable DVD suitable for storing large amounts of data. Make sure the disc is clean and scratch-free for optimal results. 10. **Write the ISO File to the DVD**: Now it's time to write the ISO image onto the prepared disc. Go back to your disc burner software and find an option labeled 'Burn Image', 'Write Disc', or similar. Select the previously created ISO file and hit 'Start'. 11. **Monitor the Writing Progress**: Keep an eye on the writing process until completion. As mentioned earlier, refrain from running heavy tasks alongside this operation. 12. **Eject the Completed DVD**: When the burning finishes successfully, eject the now bootable Windows 7 installation DVD. Label it appropriately so you know what it contains. By completing these steps, you now have a functional ISO DVD containing Windows 7 ready for installation or backup purposes! Remember to safely store the disc away from dust, heat, moisture, and direct sunlight. oh, this is A team. You'll height wait XXX [? A little]. Adding custom commands to the command prompt can be a useful way to automate tasks or simplify complex processes into a single command. This tutorial will walk you through the process of creating your own custom commands using batch files (.bat) in Windows. **Step 1: Understand Batch Files** A batch file is a type of script file that contains a series of commands meant to be run in sequence within the Command Prompt interface. These files have the extension .bat and are executed by double-clicking them or calling them from another command prompt window. Key Tip: Always ensure scripts are safe before running them, as they can make changes to system settings and delete important data if used improperly. **Step 2: Choose a Text Editor** You'll need a text editor to write your batch script. While Notepad comes preinstalled with most versions of Windows, other options like Notepad++ may offer more features such as syntax highlighting which makes reading and editing code easier. Guideline: When saving your file, remember to include the ".bat" extension after the filename so Windows recognizes it as a batch file. **Step 3: Create Your Script** Open your chosen text editor and begin writing your script. Here's an example of what a simple batch script might look like: ```bash @echo off echo Hello, World! pause ``` In this case, `@echo off` prevents the display of individual command echoes, `echo Hello, World!` prints 'Hello, World!' to the console, and `pause` holds the screen open until the user presses any key. Key Tips: * Each line should end with a carriage return. * Use comments starting with `rem` or `::` to explain what each part of the script does. For instance, `rem This line displays a message`. **Step 4: Test Your Script** Save your new batch file then navigate to its location via File Explorer or Command Prompt. Double click the file or enter its name in the command prompt to execute it. Check whether it behaves as expected. Guidelines: * Be cautious when testing scripts; some could cause unintended harm if they aren't designed correctly. * If something goes wrong, review the error messages carefully - they often provide clues about where things went awry. **Step 5: Install Your Custom Command** To add your custom command permanently, copy your newly created .bat file to one of two directories: - The current user's %AppData%\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup folder. This adds the command for the current user only. - The all users' C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\StartUp folder. This applies the change across all accounts on the machine. After placing the bat file in these folders, restart your computer or sign out and back in again for the changes to take effect. Now, whenever you open a new Command Prompt window, your custom command should be available globally. Key Tip: Make sure your batch file doesn't require administrative privileges unless necessary, as this limits who can utilize the custom command. By following these steps, you now know how to create, test, and install custom commands into your Command Prompt. Happy coding! I just thought you were going to make that line into an r. Here's what you'll need: 2 "D" cells 3 pieces of aluminum foil cut into strips 6 inches long by 1/4 inch wide 2 standard flashlight bulbs A roll of cellophane tape |Here's what to do:| |Hook up a single battery to a bulb using the aluminum foil strips:| |1. Tape one strip of foil to the positive (+) end of the battery and a second strip to the negative (-) end of the battery.| |2. Touch the free end of the positive (+) strip to the metal side of the bulb right below the glass and the free end of the negative (-) strip to the little silver tip at the bottom of the bulb.| At this point, the bulb should light! Now you're ready for the challenge. 1. What will happen to the brightness of the bulb when you add the second battery to the circuit? Try it yourself! How must the second battery be turned in order to get the bulb to light? 2. What happens to the brightness of the bulb when you add a second bulb to the circuit? Once you've got your results with the two batteries and the single bulb, use the third foil strip to insert the second bulb in the circuit. What happens to the brightness of the bulb now? Why do you think the change occurred? What happens to the bulbs if any of the connections in the circuit break? Creating a haunted house on a budget can be both fun and rewarding! With some creativity, resourcefulness, and careful planning, you can create an unforgettable experience without breaking the bank. Here's our step-by-step guide: **Step 1: Choose a Theme** Determine what kind of haunted house you want to build - is it going to have a classic horror theme with vampires and werewolves or something more unique like a creepy carnival? A clear theme will help focus your decorating efforts and make your haunted house stand out. **Step 2: Create a Floor Plan** Sketch out where different rooms or sections will go. This doesn't need to be complex; even just marking off areas using chairs, tables, etc., can work well. Consider including narrow hallways (which increase fear due to confined spaces) and dead ends. Remember that safety should always come first when designing your layout. **Step 3: Gather Materials** Before buying anything new, look around your home for items that could serve as props or d cor pieces. Old sheets, cardboard boxes, duct tape, string lights, and paint are all versatile materials that can be transformed into many things. Check thrift stores, garage sales, and online marketplaces for cheap secondhand finds too. **Step 4: Build Scary Props** Use your gathered materials to construct spooky elements such as ghostly figures made from white bedsheets draped over balloons or stuffed trash bags, cobwebs spun from yarn, and DIY tombstones crafted from foam board. Be sure to incorporate lighting effects like strobe lights or blacklights for added eeriness. **Step 5: Set Up Sound Effects** Sound adds another layer of immersion to any haunted house. You don't necessarily need expensive equipment; free sound effect apps or websites often provide enough variety. Place speakers strategically throughout the space so sounds seem to emanate from various directions. Creepy classics include creaking doors, distant screams, howling winds, and maniacal laughter. **Step 6: Design Interactive Elements** Add interactive features to keep guests engaged. For example, motion sensors could trigger sudden bursts of air or light. Or design puzzles they must solve before moving forward. These surprise interactions enhance the overall fright factor while keeping visitors entertained. **Step 7: Implement Safety Measures** Ensure there are no tripping hazards by securing wires and taping down loose corners. Clearly label emergency exits and ensure pathways remain wide enough for comfortable passage. If children will be attending, consider creating a separate route suitable for younger audiences. **Step 8: Test Your Creation** Walk through your haunted house yourself or invite friends to test it out. Their feedback can help identify weak spots or confusing areas. Make adjustments accordingly until everything flows smoothly. **Step 9: Promote Your Event** Spread the word about your haunted house via social media, local newspapers, flyers, or community bulletin boards. Encourage attendees to dress up and get into character themselves to add to the atmosphere. By following these steps, you'll be able to create a captivating haunted house that won't drain your wallet but will definitely give your guests a scare! Just remember to prioritize safety alongside creativity and enjoy the process. "Pardon! pardon!" whined the dwarf. "Oh, I have had such an awful scare!" "A scare? What is that?" asked Siegfried. I don't know if Kent remembers or not. Title: How to Buy Cheap Video Games Buying video games can be an expensive hobby, especially if you're purchasing new releases or popular titles. However, there are ways to enjoy your favorite games without breaking the bank. This guide will provide detailed steps on how to buy cheap video games while ensuring quality and value for money. Step 1: Set a Budget Before you start shopping, determine how much you're willing to spend on video games. Having a budget prevents overspending and helps you focus on finding deals within your price range. Remember that cheaper doesn't always mean better; prioritize getting good value for what you pay. Key Tip: Consider setting aside part of your entertainment budget specifically for gaming. This way, you won't feel guilty about spending on games, as long as it falls under your allocated amount. Step 2: Research Game Prices Prices vary between retailers, both online and physical stores. Spend some time researching current prices for the game(s) you want. Websites like CamelCamelCamel (for Amazon), PriceCharting, or IsThereAnyDeal can help track price histories and alert you when prices drop. Key Guideline: Don't forget to factor in shipping costs when comparing prices. Some sites may offer lower base prices but charge high shipping fees. Step 3: Utilize Sales and Discounts Wait for sales events such as Steam Summer Sale, Black Friday, Cyber Monday, or E3 discounts. These occasions often bring significant reductions on various games. Keep an eye out for daily/weekly deal offers too! Key Tip: If possible, combine sale purchases with coupon codes or rewards points to maximize savings. Step 4: Explore Alternative Platforms Consider buying digital copies from third-party sellers on platforms like Humble Bundle, Fanatical, or Green Man Gaming. They frequently have bundles, publisher sales, and seasonal promotions that significantly cut down game costs. Just ensure these sellers are reputable before making any transactions. Step 5: Check Pre-Owned Options Physical game stores like GameStop, CEX, or independent second-hand shops usually carry pre-owned games at reduced rates. Online marketplaces such as eBay, Craigslist, or Facebook Marketplace also feature used games sold by individuals. Always check condition reports and seller ratings before proceeding with such purchases. Step 6: Trade-In Old Games If you have old games lying around, trade them in at participating stores like Gamestop or sell them privately through local classifieds or online platforms. The credit earned can then be used towards buying newer or more desirable games. Step 7: Rent or Stream Games Instead of buying every single game you wish to play, consider renting them from services like GameFly or Redbox. Another option is streaming via cloud-based subscription services such as Google Stadia, NVIDIA GeForce Now, or Xbox Game Pass. Key Guideline: Make sure your internet connection is stable enough to support smooth gameplay if choosing the streaming route. Step 8: Join Communities and Forums Participate in gaming communities, either locally or online, where members share advice, news, and deals regarding cheap video games. Sites like Reddit, NeoGAF, or Cheap Ass Gamer host active boards dedicated to bargain hunting. By following these steps meticulously, you'll find great deals on video games while staying within your means. Happy gaming! Stopped long enough to shake the water from her coat. Big, Bad, b and d Emergent Literacy-Letter Recognition By Natalie Tate Rationale: It is important for beginning readers to learn how to identify letters and listen for the sounds the letters make. This lesson is designed to help students learn to recognize the difference between b=/b/ and d=/d/. These two letters look similar and sometimes the same to children. Therefore, they are often confused. During the lesson, students will have the chance to practice seeing and hearing the letters and sounds for better recognitions. Also, it is very important that children learn how letters are formed to comprehend their meaning. Through practice and direct instruction, students will be able to see and understand the difference between b and d. Materials: 1.Cards with b (blue) and d (yellow) 2. b word cards (bat, ball, boat, bell) with pictures 3. d word cards (dog, doll, duck, door) with pictures "both" words (bad, bed, bid) 5. Primary writing paper 7. Blue and yellow Cards for each student 8. Dry-erase Board 9. Dry-erase Markers 10. Book- The Berenstain Bears In the Dark, by Stan and Jan Berenstain Procedures: 1. Explain and model the day's lesson to the students. -Today we are going to learn about two letters that are at times very tricky. These letters are b (hold up b card) and d (hold up d card). Can someone raise his or her hand and tell me what sound b makes? (Student Response) Good, let's say it together /b/, /b/. Now, do you know what sound d makes? (SR) Let's say it all together now, /d/, /d/. Good job, boys and girls. Next I need a volunteer to think of some words that begin with the letter b. (SR-write on dry-erase board) Great. Now let's think of some words that start with the letter d. Who knows one? (SR-write on dry-erase board) 2. Ask the students these questions and get them to respond as a class (using blue and yellow b and d cards): "Do you hear the /b/ sound in the word bad or Dad?" "Do you hear the /d/ sound in the word drown or brown?" "Do you hear the /d/ sound in the word ball or doll?" "Do you hear the /b/ sound in the word bid or did?" 3. Go over two tongue twisters for b = /b/ and d = /d/ and write them on the board. -b = /b/ "Britney baked big brownies for the beautiful baby's birthday!" -I'll say it once and then, we will all say it together. Great job! Now let's say it one more time, emphasizing the /b/ sounds in the beginning of each word (I will hold up the blue b card on every /b/ sound.) Good work. Let's try this one now repeat after me. -d = /d/ "David dreamed that his dog dug dirt in the dark!" -Good work boys and girls! Okay, now we're going to emphasize the /d/ sounds in the beginning of each word in this tongue twister. (I will hold up yellow d card each time the /d/ sound is stressed.) 4. Practice and model letter formation and writing with students. -Now boys and girls, we are going to practice and learn the differences between b and d. Let's take out our lined writing paper and pencils. I will show you on the board how to make little b first (model as I say the phrase "For lower case b, you start at the roof and go down, bbbounce up to the fence and around.") Now you try to say it as you make your own b's on your paper. Good. Let's write 4 more lower case b's on the whole line (Allow a minute or two for students to complete). Next, boys and girls, we are going to practice writing lower case d. We all remember how to make lowercase c right? (This should be in student's prior knowledge). Watch and listen to me as I show you on the board. (Model with the phrase "For little or lower-case d, first you make a little c then little d."). Now it's your turn. Go down to the next line and say the phrase as you write the letter d. Good job. Now I want all of you to practice 4 more times writing the lowercase d on that same line. Great! 5. Connect today's lesson to the book, The Berenstain Bears In the Dark. -Boys and girls, now that you have a great comprehension of the letters b and d, we are going to see what you know by reading this book The Berenstain Bears In the Dark. Now, as I read this to you, I want you all to follow along with me. Every time you hear me read a word that begins with b = /b/, I want you to raise your blue card in the air. This means that every time you hear me say a word that has the d = /d/ sound beginning, I want you to raise your yellow card. Let's try it (Read the title and see if children understand the directions) Students will be assessed by their active participation in all parts of the lesson, but mainly focusing on the during the reading of the book. The more they participate, the better I see their comprehension of the subject matter. I will also evaluate writing practices with the letters b and d and make notes about their participation during the tongue twisters. I will also assess their individual performance on writing the letters b and d correctly. Stan and Jan. The Berenstain Bears In the Lewis, Naomi. Emergent Literacy. Today, we are going to learn about something called "Standard Normal Distribution". You may wonder, what does that even mean? Don't worry, I will try my best to make it easy to understand! Imagine you have seven friends, and each friend flips a coin ten times. Now, let's think about how many heads each friend gets. We could get anywhere between 0 heads (all tails) and 10 heads (all heads), but most of the time, we expect them to get around 5 heads (half of the tosses). But what if instead of counting the number of heads, we square the result of each flip (so getting heads becomes 1 and getting tails also becomes 1)? Then we add up all these squared results for all ten tosses for each friend. That's what our problem is asking us to do - just imagine doing this for seven friends! Now, why did we square the results? Well, by squaring the results, we focus on how far off each outcome is from the expected value (which was 5 heads). This helps us see whether someone got more or fewer heads than we expected. And when we add up the squared values for multiple friends, we create a new measure that tells us something about the group as a whole. The cool thing is, we can actually calculate the probability that the total sum falls within certain ranges using something called the Chi-Square distribution. In our case, since we added up the squares of seven outcomes, we use the Chi-Square distribution with seven degrees of freedom (we call it Chi-Square 7). With this information, we can find out the likelihood of different sums happening. In summary, today we learned about the Standard Normal Distribution and its connection to real-life situations like counting heads during coin flips. By squaring and adding up deviations from the expected value, we created a useful measure to analyze groups of data. Using the Chi-Square distribution, we can then determine probabilities associated with various sums, making statistics a powerful tool for understanding the world around us!
