Getting 1,000,000 gems can be a challenging but rewarding goal, especially if you're playing a game that involves collecting valuable resources. Here is an in-depth guide on how to get 1,000,000 gems: Step 1: Choose the Right Game Not all games offer gems as a resource, so the first step is to choose a game that does. Popular games like Clash of Clans, Dragon City, and Marvel Contest of Champions are just some examples where you can collect gems. Make sure to read reviews about the game's gem earning potential before downloading it. Step 2: Complete Daily Quests and Achievements Most games have daily quests or achievements that give out rewards when completed. These tasks vary from logging into the game every day, defeating certain enemies, building structures, or upgrading units. Completing these quests will help increase your gem count over time. Additionally, completing achievements often gives larger quantities of gems than daily quests do. Key Tip: Prioritize achievements with higher gem rewards. They might take longer to complete, but they provide more significant benefits in the end. Step 3: Participate in Events Many games host special events where players can earn exclusive items, currency, and yes - gems! During these events, there may be specific challenges or missions to finish. Be strategic about which ones you focus on; try to pick those with high gem payouts. Guideline: Remember event rules and objectives change frequently, so stay updated with announcements within the app or its social media platforms. Step 4: Save Your Gems It can be tempting to spend your hard-earned gems right away, but resist this urge. Saving them allows you to accumulate enough to make bigger purchases later. Plus, holding onto your gems means you won't miss out on any limited-time offers or sales that could potentially double or triple their value. Step 5: Connect Social Media Accounts Linking your gaming account to Facebook, Google Play, or other social media platforms usually provides bonus gems upon signup. Furthermore, connecting accounts enables you to connect with friends who also play the same game. This connection opens opportunities for trading resources, forming clans, or competing against others, resulting in extra gems as well. Step 6: Watch Advertisements Some free-to-play games show advertisements between levels or after winning battles. Watching these ads sometimes results in free gems. While it might seem tedious, think of it as passive income while enjoying your game. Step 7: Buy Them (Optional) If none of the above methods work fast enough, consider purchasing gems directly through the app store or in-game shop. Prices range depending on quantity, but keep in mind that buying large amounts at once tends to cost less per gem compared to smaller increments. However, only resort to this method if necessary, since our main objective here is getting one million gems without spending real money. Remember, patience is crucial when trying to amass such a substantial amount of gems. Follow these steps consistently, remain patient, and eventually, you'll reach your goal of obtaining 1,000,000 gems! Bumper would probably get something from the tooth fairy and there would be nothing for her. It's Thursday again, and time for another little lesson from the English teacher. Several people have asked me to address the use of I and me. These two little words are amazingly useful but are often misused. I've found that mistakes involving the use of these words is a pet peeve of many folks. I and me are both personal pronouns (words that take the place of nouns). For the most part, the use of pronouns comes very naturally to native English speakers. One troublesome point is knowing when to use subjective pronouns and when to use objective pronouns. Here are the rules for that: Use a subjective pronoun (I, you, he, she, they) as the subject of a sentence or phrase or following a linking verb (is, am, are, was, were, be). - I love chocolate. - They left town this morning. - This is she. (Think about how you learned to answer the phone!) Use an objective pronoun (me, you, him, her, them) as a direct or indirect object of a sentence or the object of a preposition. Prepositions are the connecting words used to build phrases that usually describe relationship of some kind. Some common prepositions are about, at, before, between, by, for, from, of, on, to, and with. The word or words following a preposition are the objects of the preposition. When you use a pronoun as the object of a preposition, you need an objective pronoun. - Charlie bit me! ("Me" is the direct object of bit.) - Pam made me a pillow. ("Me" is the indirect object of made.) - This book is all about her. ("Her" is the object of the preposition about.) Now, here's where it gets tricky. When you were very small, you probably said to your mom something like, "Me and Sally are going to ride our bikes." And your mom probably said, "Don't say 'me and Sally'; say 'Sally and I.'" She was trying to teach you that it's polite to say the other person's name first, then say your own name. She was right about that. And she probably had to repeat this rule of courtesy a number of times before you got it. The important part of that lesson was being polite, not using good grammar. But you need to know that you should say "Sally and I" ONLY when you need the subject of a sentence or phrase. If the words "Sally and I" are serving as the object of a phrase, then you need to switch to "Sally and me." - Sally and I are going to the movie. - Mom baked cookies for Sally and me. - Please take a picture of Sally and me. - If you have any questions, just ask Sally or me. There's an easy way to know whether to use "Sally and I" or "Sally and me." Just take "Sally" out of the sentence for a second. Would you say, "Mom baked cookies for I"? "Please take a picture of I"? Or "Just ask I"? No, of course you wouldn't; you would naturally say "me" instead of "I." So if you would naturally say "me," then you should use "me" in conjunction with the other person's name. Your mom was right: it IS polite to say the other person's name first. But choose to add "I" or "me" based on how you're using the words in a sentence. Here's an example: "Let's keep this just between you and I." Between is a preposition, so you need an object of the preposition. The correct wording is "between you and me." Here's a little quiz for you. - My husband and _____ (I or me) just celebrated our anniversary. - I love this photo of my husband and _____ (I or me) from our wedding day. If you answered "I" for number 1 and "me" for number 2, you're right! Please let me know if you have any questions about English grammar or if there's a particular point you'd like for me to address in one of these little lessons. I want these to be a blessing to you. Leave me a comment if there's a particular point of grammar you'd like for me to address. Feel free to email me (RichellaP (at) gmail (dot) com) if you have a specific question. Title: How to Backup iPod Music to a Computer Using Syncios iPod Transfer Introduction: Syncios iPod Transfer is a powerful third-party tool designed for managing, transferring, and backing up data between iOS devices (iPhone, iPad, iPod) and computers. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of backing up your iPod music to a computer using this software. By following these steps, you'll ensure that your valuable music collection remains safe and accessible even if something happens to your iPod. Step 1: Downloading and Installing Syncios iPod Transfer Before starting the backup process, download the latest version of Syncios iPod Transfer from their official website onto your Windows or macOS computer. Once downloaded, install the application by double-clicking the installation file and following the prompts. It is crucial to choose a reliable source when downloading any software to avoid potential security risks. Step 2: Connecting Your iPod to the Computer Use an Apple USB cable to connect your iPod to the computer where Syncios iPod Transfer has been installed. The connection must be secure to enable seamless communication between the two devices. Make sure both devices are turned on during this process. If prompted with a message asking whether to trust the connected device, select 'Yes' or 'Trust'. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Keep your iTunes updated to prevent compatibility issues with Syncios iPod Transfer. * Avoid disconnecting your iPod while the backup operation is ongoing as it may lead to unexpected errors or corrupted files. * Use only original Apple cables or certified MFi (Made For iPhone/iPad) cables to establish connections. Step 3: Launching Syncios iPod Transfer Locate the Syncios icon on your desktop or within your applications folder and launch the program. Upon successful initialization, the main interface should appear displaying various options like Media, Photos, Ebooks, Apps, etc. Step 4: Selecting the Music Category Click on the 'Media' tab located at the top of the screen. A submenu containing different media categories such as Music, Videos, Ringtones, Podcasts, TV Shows, and others will then show up. Choose 'Music' to proceed further. Step 5: Initiating the Backup Process After selecting the 'Music' category, all songs present in your iPod library will become visible inside the Syncios iPod Transfer window. Here, you can either back up individual tracks or the entire music library. To do so, tick mark the checkbox(es) corresponding to the song(s) you wish to save. Alternatively, click on the square box beside 'Name' to select all available items. Then click the export button ('Computer') usually found at the upper part of the window. A new dialogue box will pop up requesting you to specify the destination folder on your computer where the backed-up audio files should reside. Navigate through your directories until you reach your preferred location, then press the 'OK' button. Wait patiently for the progress bar to complete indicating a successful backup. Remember, larger libraries might take longer depending on your system specifications and internet speed. Conclusion: By completing the above steps meticulously, you have now ensured that your precious iPod music collection is safely stored on your personal computer. Regularly performing such backups ensures minimal loss in case of accidental deletion, hardware failure, or theft. Stay tuned for more guides about managing your digital assets efficiently! Title: Cope with Listening to Other People Having Fun When You Are Lony Introduction: It's natural to feel lonely or left out when you see other people having fun, especially in this age of social media where everything is broadcasted online. However, there are ways to cope with these feelings without letting them consume you. This guide will provide you with steps and techniques that can help you manage your emotions and improve your overall well-being. Step 1: Acknowledge Your Feelings Recognize that what you're feeling is valid and normal. It's okay to be envious or sad when you see others enjoying themselves while you're alone. Don't suppress or ignore these emotions; instead, accept them as they come. By acknowledging your feelings, you take the first step towards addressing them effectively. Key Tip: Remember that everyone has moments of loneliness, even those who seem to have perfect lives. Social media often portrays an idealized version of reality, so try not to compare yourself too harshly to others. Step 2: Practice Self-Compassion Be kind to yourself during these times. Talk to yourself as if you were talking to a friend - offer reassurance, understanding, and encouragement. Engage in self-care activities such as taking a warm bath, reading a book, or practicing mindfulness meditation. These actions can help reduce stress and promote positive thinking. Guideline: Make a list of self-compassionate phrases that resonate with you (e.g., "I am doing my best," "This feeling will pass," "Everyone experiences loneliness at some point"). Repeat these phrases whenever you notice negative thoughts creeping in. Step 3: Connect with Others Reach out to friends, family members, or acquaintances for a conversation or shared activity. Sometimes, simply connecting with another person can alleviate feelings of loneliness. If face-to-face interaction isn't possible, consider using technology to stay connected through video calls, messaging apps, or social media platforms. Key Tip: Be proactive about making plans with others. Instead of waiting for invitations, reach out and suggest an outing or virtual hangout session. Step 4: Develop New Hobbies or Skills Engage in activities that bring you joy and fulfillment. Learning something new or pursuing a hobby can boost your confidence, expand your social circle, and distract you from dwelling on negative thoughts. Look for local classes, clubs, or groups related to your interests. Guideline: Set realistic goals for yourself when starting a new hobby. Break down larger tasks into smaller ones to avoid becoming overwhelmed and discouraged. Step 5: Seek Professional Help If feelings of loneliness persist despite your efforts to address them, consider seeking professional help. Therapists and counselors are trained to assist individuals in managing their emotions and improving mental health. They can provide guidance, support, and coping strategies tailored specifically to your needs. Key Tip: Many therapists now offer teletherapy sessions, which allow you to receive treatment remotely via phone or video call. Conclusion: Remember, it's perfectly normal to experience moments of loneliness when listening to other people having fun. However, by acknowledging your feelings, practicing self-compassion, connecting with others, developing new hobbies, and seeking professional help when necessary, you can learn to cope more effectively with these emotions and foster greater happiness and satisfaction in your life. Deduction is a special way of thinking to discover and prove new truths using old truths. To a mathematician, the reason something is true (called a proof) is just as important as the fact that it is true, and this reason is often found using deduction. Using deduction is what makes mathematics thinking different from other kinds of scientific thinking, which might rely on experiments or on interviews. He'll pretend to put her helmet on. Title: How to Establish an Athletic Drug Testing Policy Introduction: Drug testing is a critical component of maintaining a fair and safe athletic environment. It ensures that all athletes are competing on equal terms, free from performance-enhancing substances. This guide will walk you through establishing an effective drug testing policy for your athletic organization. Step 1: Define the Purpose and Scope Clarify why you're implementing this policy (e.g., to maintain fairness, ensure safety) and who it applies to (all athletes, specific teams). This helps create a solid foundation for the rest of the policy. Key Tip: Be transparent about the reasons behind the policy to gain support from athletes, coaches, and parents. Step 2: Specify Which Substances Are Prohibited Identify which drugs are banned based on reputable sources like the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) or NCAA. Include categories such as stimulants, anabolic agents, diuretics, and masking agents. Guideline: Regularly update the list of prohibited substances to reflect changes in regulations and emerging trends in doping. Step 3: Outline Testing Protocols Describe when and how tests will be conducted, including random selection methods and types of samples collected (urine, blood). Also, specify whether testing will occur in-competition, out-of-competition, or both. Key Tip: Implement random testing to discourage athletes from attempting to cheat the system. Step 4: Detail Consequences for Violations Clearly outline penalties for first-, second-, and third-time offenses, up to and including suspension or expulsion from the team or program. Make sure these consequences align with any governing body rules. Guideline: Provide opportunities for education and rehabilitation before imposing severe punishments. Step 5: Establish Confidentiality and Privacy Protections Guarantee confidentiality during the testing process and when handling results. Adhere to local laws regarding privacy rights and share information only on a need-to-know basis. Key Tip: Appoint a designated individual to manage test scheduling and result dissemination to protect athlete identities. Step 6: Develop Education and Awareness Programs Educate athletes, coaches, and staff about the dangers of performance-enhancing drugs and the importance of clean sport. Offer resources and workshops to help them understand the policy and its implications. Guideline: Encourage open communication between athletes, coaches, and medical personnel to address concerns and questions about supplement usage. Step 7: Secure Necessary Funding and Resources Determine budget requirements for equipment, laboratory analysis, personnel, and educational materials. Seek financial support from school administration, sponsors, or other relevant organizations. Key Tip: Allocate sufficient funds to ensure accurate testing and reliable results. Step 8: Monitor and Review the Policy Regularly assess the effectiveness of the policy by tracking violations, monitoring feedback from athletes and stakeholders, and adjusting procedures as needed. Guideline: Periodically consult experts in sports medicine, anti-doping agencies, and legal counsel to stay informed about best practices and regulatory updates. Conclusion: Implementing a comprehensive drug testing policy requires careful planning, clear communication, and consistent enforcement. By following these steps, you can promote a level playing field, foster integrity within your athletic community, and prioritize the health and well-being of all participants. Title: How to Connect Your LinkedIn Account to oDesk Introduction: Linking your LinkedIn account to oDesk can provide numerous benefits such as enhancing your professional profile, expanding your network, and increasing your chances of getting hired by potential clients. This tutorial will guide you through the process of connecting your LinkedIn account to oDesk (now known as Upwork) with clear and concise steps. Step 1: Log into your oDesk account Visit <https://www.upwork.com/> and log into your existing oDesk account using your email address and password. If you don't have an oDesk account yet, create one first before proceeding. Having an active oDesk account is necessary to connect it with LinkedIn. Step 2: Navigate to Settings After logging in, click on your profile picture at the top right corner of the page, then select 'Settings' from the dropdown menu. This section contains all your personal information, preferences, payment settings, and connected accounts. Step 3: Access the Connected Accounts Section From the left sidebar within Settings, locate and click on 'Connected Accounts.' This area allows users to link their social media profiles like LinkedIn, Facebook, or Twitter to their oDesk account. Step 4: Link LinkedIn Account You should now see the option to 'Link another account,' followed by several social media platform options. Click on the LinkedIn icon to start the connection process. A pop-up window requesting authorization will appear. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Make sure you are logged into both your oDesk and LinkedIn accounts in different browser tabs to avoid any inconvenience during the authentication process. - Always review what permissions you grant when authenticating third-party apps with your LinkedIn account. Only allow access to essential features required for seamless integration between platforms. Step 5: Authorize oDesk App on LinkedIn The pop-up window requires you to enter your LinkedIn login credentials if you aren't already logged in. After entering your details, click 'Allow Access' to give permission for oDesk to access your LinkedIn profile data. Important Note: Be cautious about providing sensitive information or consenting to actions that might compromise your online security. Only approve requests from reputable sources like oDesk (Upwork). Step 6: Confirm Connection Successful Once authorized successfully, you will be redirected back to the Connected Accounts page on oDesk, where you should now see LinkedIn listed under 'Your Connected Accounts.' You may also notice some additional information pulled from your LinkedIn profile, such as your name, title, location, industry, and public profile URL. Congratulations! You have successfully linked your LinkedIn account to your oDesk profile. By doing so, you increase visibility among potential clients searching for freelancers with specific skills or experiences showcased on LinkedIn. Additionally, this connection simplifies importing contacts and sharing job opportunities across both networks. Remember always to keep your oDesk and LinkedIn profiles updated and consistent regarding education, work experience, skills, and achievements. An accurate representation of yourself across multiple platforms strengthens credibility and boosts your overall professional image. Tom and Lily were twins who loved to look at the stars. Every night, they would go outside with their dad and point at the different shapes and colors in the sky. One night, their dad told them something special was going to happen. He said a comet was coming, and it would be very bright and fast. "What is a comet, dad?" Tom asked. "A comet is like a big ball of ice and dust that flies around the sun. Sometimes, we can see it from the earth when it gets close enough. It has a long tail that glows in the dark," their dad explained. "Wow, that sounds cool! Can we see it tonight?" Lily asked. "Yes, we can, but we have to prepare first. We need to get our coats, hats, gloves, and blankets, because it will be very cold outside. We also need to get our binoculars, so we can see the comet better. And we need to find a good spot, where there are no lights or trees to block our view," their dad said. Tom and Lily ran to get their things, and followed their dad to the backyard. They set up their blankets on the grass, and waited for the comet to appear. Their dad checked his watch, and said it was almost time. "Look, there it is!" he shouted, pointing at the sky. Tom and Lily looked up, and saw a bright streak of light zooming across the stars. It was the comet, and it was beautiful. They grabbed their binoculars, and tried to see its tail and its colors. "Wow, it's so amazing!" Tom said. "It's like a shooting star, but bigger!" Lily said. They watched the comet for a few minutes, until it faded away. They felt happy and amazed, and hugged their dad. "Thank you, dad, for showing us the comet. It was the best thing ever!" they said. "You're welcome, my little stars. I'm glad you enjoyed it. Now, let's go inside and have some hot chocolate. It's time for bed," their dad said. They packed their things, and went inside. They drank their hot chocolate, and brushed their teeth. They put on their pajamas, and crawled into their beds. Title: How to Clean Puppies After Delivery Introduction: Bringing new life into the world is an exciting experience, but it also comes with responsibilities. One such responsibility is ensuring that newborn puppies are cleaned properly after delivery. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to clean puppies after birth, helping them stay healthy and comfortable during their crucial first days. Step 1: Wait for Placenta Expulsion After each puppy is born, wait until its mother expels the placenta before proceeding with cleaning. The placenta provides essential nutrients to the puppy while in the womb; therefore, interfering too soon might disrupt this natural process. However, if the mother doesn't expel the placenta within 15 minutes or shows signs of discomfort (such as straining), gently pull it out by holding onto the umbilical cord close to the pup's belly. Step 2: Cutting the Umbilical Cord Using a pair of clean scissors sterilized with alcohol, cut the umbilical cord about one inch away from the puppy's abdomen. Be careful not to tug at the cord during this procedure. After cutting, dip your finger in iodine solution and dab it on the stump to prevent infection. Repeat this every time a new puppy is born. Key Tip: Use blunt-ended scissors to reduce the risk of accidentally injuring the puppy. Step 3: Stimulating Bowel Movements Rubbing the genital area gently can help stimulate the puppy to urinate and defecate. You may notice meconium - the puppy's first stool, which is dark and sticky. If the puppy does not pass meconium within the first 24 hours, consult a vet as this could indicate constipation. Step 4: Drying Off Use a soft towel or cloth to dry off the puppy. Make sure to pat gently rather than rubbing harshly, as the latter could cause harm to their delicate skin. Keeping the puppy warm is vital at this stage, so consider using a heating pad set on low heat wrapped in a blanket. Step 5: Weighing the Pup Weighing the puppy allows tracking growth progress over time. Using a kitchen scale, weigh the puppy naked and record the weight. Newborn pups typically lose around 10% of their body weight in the first few days post-delivery due to loss of fluids, but they should regain this weight by day ten. Step 6: Monitor Breathing Check whether the puppy is breathing regularly - about once every two seconds. Clear mucus from nostrils carefully with a cotton ball dampened in warm water if necessary. Persistent irregularities in breathing warrant immediate veterinary attention. Guideline: A normal respiratory rate ranges between 15 to 40 breaths per minute. Conclusion: Properly cleaning puppies immediately following birth ensures hygiene, promotes bonding, and contributes significantly to the overall health of both the mom and her litter. The sky is blue due to a phenomenon called Rayleigh scattering. Light is scattered off of the molecules in the atmosphere. When the white light from the sun is scattered, the sky appears blue as the wavelength of blue light is on the shorter side of the visible spectrum. The sun appears yellow as blue light and yellow light combine to make white light. At sunrise and sunset, the light is also scattered, but since the sun is low on the horizon, the light is scattered over a longer distance and through more of the atmosphere. The scattered blue light at that time falls outside a viewer's line of site. Yellow, red and orange have longer wavelengths, and those are the colors you will see at sunrise and sunset. Heat Capacity During Transitions: A Grade School Explanation ----------------------------------------------------------- Imagine you have two thermos flasks (or soda cans) connected by a metal rod. You know that one thermos contains hot chocolate at 50 C (122 F), while the other has ice tea at 10 C (50 F). Over time, the hot chocolate will start to cool down, sharing its heat with the ice tea through the connecting rod. Eventually, both drinks will reach the same middle temperature, achieving something called "steady state." However, we want to understand the concept of **heat capacity** before steady state happens - when the hot drink is still transitioning towards equilibrium. So let us simplify our setup further! ### Setting Up Our System Let's focus on just the hot chocolate inside its thermos, represented as Node A. We also need some information about the material and shape of the connection between the thermoses; imagine it as a solid steel bar. Let's call the combined impact of the material type and shape factor "$k$". As time passes ($t$), the hot chocolate's temperature decreases ($T(t)$) because it's losing heat to the ice tea via conduction along the steel bar. Since there's less heat remaining in the hot chocolate over time, we can say that its *rate of heat loss* ($\frac{{dE}}{{dt}}$)* equals $-k*(T(t)-30)$, where 30 C represents the stable temperature of the ice tea. Remember, negative signs indicate decreasing values. Now here comes the crucial part: **Heat Content**. It tells us how much heat is stored within an object based on its current temperature and its own specific heat capacity ($C_h$). In our case, the heat content equation looks like ${E_h}=C_h*{T}$. So now you may wonder... > *"How does knowing all this help me find out the heat capacity ($C_h$) of my hot chocolate?"* Well, dear curious student, patience pays off! By setting up a simple mathematical relationship between our variables, we'll get closer to answering your question. --- *Note: At this stage, avoid getting intimidated by complex math symbols or formulas mentioned earlier. Instead, grasp the main idea behind these concepts. A little egg lay on a leaf. yeah, but you know if you found... How're you going to get it off if I put it on? Do you want to put ketchup on it or some XXX on the ham? Do you want the box from the bakery? Sure let's do it the easy way. And I'm pretty sure she tried to imitate me. Title: How to Become a Popular Singer Introduction: Becoming a popular singer requires a combination of talent, hard work, dedication, and strategic planning. This guide will walk you through the steps needed to become a successful singer, providing in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines along the way. Remember that success may not come overnight, but with persistence and patience, you can make your dreams a reality. Step 1: Develop Your Vocal Skills Explanation: Before attempting to become a professional singer, it is crucial to hone your vocal skills. A strong voice and excellent technique are essential foundations for any successful singing career. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Take private singing lessons from a qualified vocal coach who can help improve pitch, breath control, range, and tone. * Practice daily, focusing on exercises that target specific areas of improvement. * Record yourself while practicing to track progress and identify areas needing more attention. * Learn proper warm-up techniques to prevent damage to your vocal cords. * Sing various genres of music to expand your versatility as a performer. Step 2: Discover Your Unique Sound Explanation: Finding your unique sound involves identifying your strengths as a vocalist and developing a personal style that sets you apart from other artists. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Experiment with different musical styles to discover which genre(s) best suit your voice and preferences. * Listen to diverse musicians for inspiration, but avoid imitating them entirely; instead, incorporate elements into your own sound. * Collaborate with other musicians and producers to explore new ideas and broaden your artistic vision. * Embrace your natural voice rather than trying to force an unnatural sound or style. Step 3: Build a Strong Musical Foundation Explanation: To become a well-rounded musician, learn to play an instrument (such as guitar or piano), understand music theory, and develop your songwriting abilities. These skills will enhance your performances and enable you to contribute more effectively during recording sessions and collaborations. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Enroll in courses or hire a tutor to teach you music theory and instrumental skills. * Dedicate time each day to practice playing instruments and writing songs. * Analyze hit songs within your preferred genre(s) to understand their structure, harmony, melody, and lyrics. * Collaborate with other songwriters to refine your craft and gain fresh perspectives. Step 4: Create a Professional Portfolio You're wearing a very pretty dress in that picture. Modern cars have brakes on all four wheels. No brakes are on the front wheels of a Model T. Title: Solving Fun Data Point Puzzles with Python! Hi there, grade-schoolers! Today we're going to learn about a cool puzzle involving data points and using Python to solve it. Let's say you have different sets of data points, like Set 1 has 5 points, Set 2 has 1 point, Set 3 has 10 points, and so on. Your task is to pick just ONE point from each set, making sure the total distance between all chosen points is as small as possible. Sounds fun, doesn't it? To better understand this problem, let's think about picking apples in an orchard! You want to visit every apple tree section (set) once and collect just one apple per section while walking the shortest path. This way, you save time AND energy! Now, wouldn't that make your teacher proud? Let's dive into simplified math concepts behind this puzzle. We'll call our selected apple locations "variables" which can either be 0 (not picked) or 1 (picked). Our goal is to minimize adding up the distances between pairs of chosen apple trees - after all; we don't want to walk extra steps! Now comes the exciting part - solving this puzzle with Python! While you may not know Python yet, imagine having magical functions helping you find the smallest path in no time. That would be amazing, huh? As grade-schoolers, you already possess fantastic skills to grasp programming ideas, especially when linked to real-life problems! So who knows, maybe next year, you could start coding this solution yourself! Remember, learning new things takes practice and patience, but soon enough, you too can master incredible challenges like this one. Happy puzzling! Lily and Tom were friends who liked to play with sand. They had a big bucket and a small shovel. They wanted to make an island with sand. They filled the bucket with sand and turned it over on the ground. They made a round hill of sand. "Look, we made an island!" Lily said. "But it needs something more." "Maybe we can make a palm tree with leaves and a coconut," Tom said. He found a stick and some green paper. He cut the paper into leaf shapes and stuck them on the stick. He also found a brown ball and put it on the stick. He pushed the stick into the sand hill. "Wow, that looks like a real palm tree!" Lily said. "But how can we get the coconut?" Tom had an idea. He climbed on the sand hill and reached for the ball. He pulled it off the stick and held it in his hands. He squeezed the ball hard. "Crack!" The ball broke into two pieces. Inside, there was white stuff and water. "Look, it's a real coconut!" Tom said. He gave one piece to Lily and kept the other. They drank the water and ate the white stuff. "Mmm, this is yummy!" Lily said. "We are successful island makers!" They smiled and hugged each other. They were happy and proud of their island. They played with sand until it was time to go home. Spool with two strings attached to a handle To start make sure that the string is wound around the inner spool with about two feet left out for you to be able to pull on. For the first part of the demonstration pull on the rope while it is parallel or at a small angle to the ground, the spool should roll towards you (right on the diagram). Next you will pull the rope directly up, perpendicular to the ground, and the spool should roll away from you (left on the diagram). The last part of the demonstration requires that you line up the rope so that the line made by the rope points directly at the point of contact between the spool and ground. When you pull on the rope, the spool should slide (not roll) towards you. To make sure that all the three examples will work for you, try them before class. The sliding spool is the hardest to perform and therefore will require extra practice to make them work. You also want to be careful if you are performing this demonstration on a table because the spool will continue to roll of the table and may be damaged. Each example can be counter intuitive so you may wish to poll the class on which way the spool will roll. The class will likely require a detailed explanation using the torque about the point of contact. Consider the torque about the point of contact between the spool and ground. In the first example rxF where r is drawn from the point of contact (point A) to where the string leaves the inner spool (point B), is into the page. In the second example rxF is out of the page In the third example rxF is equal to 0 so the torque is 0 and the spool slides. Consider the net Force in each case. In the first example any vertical component of the tension is balanced with gravity and the normal force of the table. Since the spool rolls to the right, the force of friction is also to the right. So the net force is to the right. In the second example, again gravity, the normal force and the vertical string tension add to zero. Since the spool rolls to the left, the frictional force is to the left. Since you give no horizontal string tension, it is the force of friction that causes the spool to move to the left. In the third example, the friction is to the left, but it is overcome by the horizontal component of the string tension which is to the right I think that's as far as you can go. The agriculture industry in BC is, and always has been humble. However, as they say, good things come in small packages, and the agricultural goods of BC, are about as good as they come. Due to the fact that BC has only about 2% arable land, the remainder being too dry, or too cold, or too remote, or a plethora of other reasons, BC has never been able to sustain a primary food agricultural industry. Even today, BC cannot grow enough food to feed its entire population, and imports foods instead, from the eastern provinces, such as Alberta, and Saskatchewan. However, where BC does grow food, the food is scrumptious, and award-winning. The Okanagan valley, is known for its award-winning fruits, and wine-related products. However, that is not where all the food is produced. In the Fraser valley delta, large amounts of other food crops are grown, such as wheat, and berries. yeah, we don't have like straight up wooden blocks that he plays with at this point. Doubleshotting is a technique used by players in Halo 2 to quickly fire two shots from a weapon that typically only allows for one shot at a time before needing to reload. This can be especially useful with weapons like the sniper rifle or battle rifle, as it allows you to deal more damage in a shorter amount of time. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to doubleshot in Halo 2: 1. **Choose your weapon:** Not all weapons in Halo 2 are capable of being doubleshot. The most popular choices for doubleshotting are the sniper rifle and the battle rifle. These weapons have a longer delay between shots, making it easier to squeeze in an extra shot before the weapon has fully cycled. 2. **Aim down sights:** Before firing, make sure to aim down the sight of your weapon. This will help ensure that both of your shots are accurate and hit your target. It also helps reduce the recoil of the weapon, which can throw off your second shot if you're not careful. 3. **Fire your first shot:** Once you have aimed down the sight, go ahead and fire your first shot. Make sure to hold down the trigger until the shot has been fired completely. 4. **Quickly release and press the trigger again:** As soon as your first shot has been fired, quickly release the trigger and then immediately press it again. This should allow you to fire a second shot before the weapon has had a chance to cycle completely. 5. **Practice, practice, practice:** Doubleshotting takes a lot of practice to get right. Don't expect to master the technique overnight. Keep practicing, and over time you'll start to get a feel for when you can squeeze in that second shot. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Be mindful of your ammo count. Doubleshotting uses up twice as many bullets as normal shooting, so make sure you have enough ammo to spare. * Pay attention to your reticle. When you're doubleshotting, your reticle may bounce around a bit due to the rapid firing of the weapon. Try to keep it steady and focused on your target. * Aim for weak spots. If you're using a weapon like the sniper rifle, try to aim for weak points on your enemy's body such as the head or chest. This will maximize the damage dealt by your doubleshot. * Use cover effectively. Since doubleshotting leaves you vulnerable for a brief period while you rapidly fire your weapon, make sure to use cover effectively to avoid getting hit by return fire. * Practice against bots. If you're having trouble getting the hang of doubleshotting, try practicing against AI bots in custom games. They're less unpredictable than human opponents, and you can set the difficulty level to something that suits your skill level. * Watch replays of pro players. One of the best ways to learn doubleshotting (and other advanced techniques) is to watch replays of professional players who excel at these skills. You can find plenty of replays online, and they can provide valuable insights into how to execute complex maneuvers like doubleshotting. See you have to slide on this car. Title: How to Highlight Duplicates on Google Sheets on PC or Mac Google Sheets is a powerful tool that allows you to organize, analyze, and share data. One common task when working with large datasets is identifying duplicate values. This tutorial will guide you through the process of highlighting duplicates in Google Sheets using your PC or Mac. By the end of this article, you'll be able to manage your data more efficiently by quickly spotting and handling duplicate entries. ### Section 1: Preparation Before we begin, ensure that you have access to a Google account and are logged into Google Drive. You can create a new Google Sheet or open an existing one for practice. Having your data prepared and saved on Google Drive will make following this tutorial much easier. **Tip**: Remember to save your work regularly as you progress through the steps. ### Section 2: Selecting Data 1. Open your Google Sheets document. Locate the dataset where you want to find duplicates. To select the entire range, click and drag your cursor across the cells containing the data. Alternatively, click on the first cell, then hold down 'Shift' while clicking on the last cell of your dataset. **Key Guideline**: Make sure all columns with potentially duplicate information are included within the selected range. Including unnecessary columns may slow down the process unnecessarily. ### Section 3: Conditional Formatting 2. Navigate to the 'Format' menu at the top of the page. Hover over 'Conditional formatting.' A drop-down list will appear. 3. From the drop-down list, choose 'Custom formula is.' A small dialog box labeled 'Enter a custom formula' will pop up. Here, we will input our criteria for finding duplicates. 4. Enter the following formula exactly as shown below (including the dollar signs) into the dialog box: `=countif($A$1:$A$<last row>, $A1)>1` Replace "<last row>" with the number corresponding to the last row of your selected data range. For example, if your data ends at row 50, replace "<last row>" with "50". The final formula should look like this: `=countif($A$1:$A$50, $A1)>1` This formula checks whether there is more than one occurrence of the value in column A throughout rows 1 to 50. If so, it highlights the cell accordingly. **Key Tip**: Be careful when entering the formula. Double-check that you have entered the correct range and column reference ($A1). Using absolute references (with the dollar sign) ensures that conditional formatting applies consistently even if you copy or extend the format to other cells. ### Section 4: Applying Styles 5. After entering the formula, specify the formatting options by selecting the 'Formatting style' section under the same dialog box. Choose any preferred color from the fill color picker for highlighting the duplicate cells. Click 'Done' once complete. 6. Your selected data range should now display highlighted cells indicating duplicate values based on the specified conditions. **Key Guideline**: Review the results carefully. Adjust the formula if necessary, ensuring accuracy before proceeding with further analysis or decision making. Congratulations! You have successfully learned how to highlight duplicates on Google Sheets using a PC or Mac. With this skill, managing and analyzing large datasets becomes significantly more accessible and efficient. Okay well put it with the other cup. And then give me back that [referent unclear]... " At certain times of the year, a message from Mars traveling at the speed of light can take 20 minutes to arrive at Earth. As if that weren't bad enough, space radiation may flip one or more bits, where flipping means turning a 1 into a 0 or vice versa. For this reason, you've been called in to help with the coding. Here is what you are told. The spacecraft will send a bunch of messages, each consisting of 100 bits plus some extra bits as we discuss below. There is an infrequent burst of space noise that can cause one or more consecutive bits to flip. The consecutive sequence of flipped bits will have a length of at most 20. Moreover, those bursts will happen at most once in the time it takes to transmit 150 bits. We'll work up to that noisiest case in stages. Suppose the noise burst could flip at most one bit in the time it takes to transmit 150. How few bits could you add to the message to detect the presence of an error? Just one extra bit would do it. Count the number of 1s among the 100-bit message. Call that number N. Add an extra bit (the 101st) with a value 1 if N is even and make the extra bit 0 if N is odd. (This is called "odd parity" in the electronic trade.) When the message (along with the extra bit) arrives at earth, there has been an error if the number of 1s among the 101 bits is even. Otherwise there is no error. 1. Next, let's assume that the noise burst will flip EXACTLY 20 consecutive bits or will flip no bits at all. How few extra bits would you then need to detect the presence of an error in the message? Okay, if you've solved that, let's try for the full problem. 2. There can be zero or one noise burst in the time it takes to transmit 150 bits. If there is a noise burst, it will flip some consecutive sequence of at most 20 bits. How few extra bits would you then need to detect the presence of an error in the message? (Hint: I can do it with fewer than 115.) Here is an open question. How many bits would be necessary to determine where such a noisy consecutive sequence of bit flips begins and ends in the setting of question 2? How would you do it? "Ching get lettee flom fliend." Can we take these [objects on silver men] off? Middle school children often focus on their faults, or what they think are their faults. In their minds, they are not attractive enough, not tall enough, not good enough in sports, or not smart enough. A little of this is normal. But too much can lead to long-term poor self-esteem. Help your child see herself as a person of value: - Help her find her gift. Some children are good at math, some are good musicians, others are good writers. - Take an interest in the things that are important to her. Don't make light of things that she takes seriously. - Offer specific praise. Not just, "You're great," but, "That dessert you made last week was delicious." - Treat her the way you want her to treat you-with kindness and respect. Never be sarcastic to your child. - Encourage her to volunteer. Helping others often makes people realize how much they have in their own lives. It is also a great way to feel good about yourself. - Notice effort. Like anyone else, your child will not be able to do everything she tries. But trying hard at something is an achievement in itself. It deserves to be recognized. - Let her have a bad day. On some days, your child will be too upset or sad or preoccupied to do her best. Offer a hug and a word of comfort. - Be there to listen. Make sure your child knows you will always have time to hear whatever is on her mind. Being listened to is an important human need. A lump of metal can be beaten into a thin sheet (it is malleable) or can be pulled into thin wires (it is ductile). Metal is hard to pull apart (it has a high tensile strength) or smash (it has a high compressive strength). If you push on a long, thin piece of metal, it will bend, not break (it is elastic). Title: How to Help Your Brother Get a Date Introduction: Dating can be a challenging experience for many people, including your brother. As a sibling, you have a unique opportunity to provide support and guidance during this time. This tutorial will walk you through various steps to help your brother get a date, along with detailed explanations, key tips, and guidelines. Step 1: Understand His Preferences Begin by having an open conversation with your brother about his preferences in a partner. Ask questions such as: What qualities does he find attractive? Does he prefer someone outgoing or introverted? Are there any specific hobbies or interests that he'd like his potential partner to share? By understanding these factors, you can better assist him in finding compatible individuals. Key Tip: Be respectful and non-judgmental throughout the conversation. It is essential to create a safe space where your brother feels comfortable sharing personal information. Step 2: Encourage Self-Improvement Suggest activities that promote self-confidence and overall well-being. Exercise, grooming, and learning new skills are all excellent ways to boost self-esteem and make your brother more appealing to others. Additionally, pursuing hobbies allows him to meet like-minded individuals who may become romantic interests. Guideline: Remember to approach this topic gently. Emphasize the benefits of self-improvement beyond dating, so your brother doesn't feel pressured or criticized. Step 3: Teach Social Skills If necessary, offer advice on making small talk, active listening, and expressing emotions effectively. These skills will enable your brother to build stronger connections with potential partners. Consider role-playing scenarios to practice these interactions in a low-stakes environment. Key Tip: Focus on genuine interest and empathy when teaching social skills. Demonstrate how showing sincere curiosity towards others fosters meaningful conversations and relationships. Step 4: Utilize Online Dating Platforms Assist your brother in creating an engaging online dating profile. Highlight his strengths, accomplishments, and passions. Use recent photos that accurately represent his appearance and personality. Research different platforms catering to specific interests or communities if applicable. Guideline: Review the privacy settings together to ensure your brother's safety while using these services. Also, discuss appropriate communication boundaries and red flags to watch out for in online interactions. Step 5: Organize Group Outings Plan group events involving friends, family members, or colleagues. Invite people from diverse backgrounds and age groups to increase the likelihood of meeting suitable matches. Activities such as game nights, picnics, or movie screenings encourage mingling and foster organic connections between attendees. Key Tip: Suggest icebreaker games or conversation starters to ease initial introductions and alleviate pressure on your brother. Step 6: Provide Constructive Feedback After social engagements, ask your brother about his experiences and offer gentle feedback if requested. Discuss successful strategies used during conversations and areas needing improvement. Maintain a positive tone, focusing on growth rather than criticism. Guideline: Always obtain consent before offering unsolicited advice. Respect your brother's autonomy and allow him to navigate his own path towards forming relationships. Conclusion: Helping your brother get a date involves understanding his preferences, encouraging self-improvement, teaching social skills, utilizing online dating platforms, organizing group outings, and providing constructive feedback. By following these steps with care, sensitivity, and respect, you can significantly impact your brother's journey toward finding love. You're growin' out of your clothes, huh? Customizing your Skype theme can enhance your user experience by making the platform more visually appealing and comfortable to use. But now that they're in high school, Carlos finds himself relying on Cecilia's notes to do well on tests. He also finds himself wondering how Cecilia manages to take such good notes. Note-taking is a skill that can help you do well on all your schoolwork - everything from taking tests to researching a paper. But unfortunately, most schools don't have classes that teach you how to take notes. So here are some tips. Write down key facts. If you have a teacher who writes notes on the board, that's a bonus: You can copy them down. If not, write down the most important points from class. Does your history teacher mention the date of a key Civil War battle? Does your English teacher give examples of Shakespeare's use of dramatic irony? Does your math teacher go over a particular formula? Write it down! It can take some experimenting to figure out what information really is helpful, so keep trying and don't give up. Different teachers do things differently. For example, some teachers may mention lots of dates and facts in class but only write the key ones on the board. Other teachers may not write anything down, but they may repeat a certain date or piece of information. That's a clue that it's probably important. After a while, you'll get to know a teacher's style. Don't overdo it. Don't go crazy taking notes, though: You'll be frantic if you try to write down every word that's said in class. And if you focus too much on getting your notes right, you might miss important points. Some people actually learn better by listening, writing down a few key points, and then going over the material after class when they have more time. Ask. Don't be afraid to ask the teacher to repeat something you miss. If the teacher's going too fast, chances are your classmates will also be relieved to hear the information again. If you don't want to ask in class, see your teacher afterwards. It's much easier than wondering if you got the notes right as you study. Compare. Keep your notes handy when you're doing your reading assignments. Compare what you wrote with what the readings say - you may even want to add to your notes as you read. Going over your notes with a friend and comparing what the two of you put down can help reinforce what you're learning. It also can help you remember information when it's time for the test. And going over your notes will alert you and your friend to any errors. Copy. Depending on how neat your handwriting is, you may want to recopy your notes when you get home. If you've taken notes in a hurry, you're more likely to figure out an unreadable word or sentence on the day of the lesson than you are weeks later when you look back over your notes in preparation for a test. Organize. Keep notes for each subject in one place so you can find everything easily when it comes time for a test. That may mean keeping a notebook or section of a notebook for each subject as you take notes in class. Some people combine the copying technique with organization by using just one notebook for class notes and then copying these notes into a notebook for each subject when they get back home. The trick to making this technique work is to be sure you actually do it regularly. If you don't, your notes will be all over the place and things will get totally crazed when it's time to study for the test. Good note-taking requires extra time and organization. It may help if you think of the time you spend reviewing notes as an investment. For example, if you decide to recopy your notes each evening, you'll probably have less time to watch TV or IM friends. But you'll save time later when it comes to studying for the actual test. Note-taking gives your mind a chance to absorb the material it needs to learn. Not only can this help you to do better on a test, it's also a great confidence booster when you're studying and find yourself saying, "Hey, I remember that!" Title: How to Group Facebook Friends on Android Facebook allows you to group your friends into different categories or lists, making it easier to share posts with specific groups of people. This feature is particularly useful if you want to share personal updates with family members only, professional content with colleagues, or keep your gaming friends separate from acquaintances. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to group Facebook friends on an Android device. **Step 1:** Open the **Facebook App** on your Android device. Tap on the three horizontal lines (hamburger menu) located at the top right corner of the screen. This will open up the navigation drawer where all the options are listed. Key Tip: Make sure that you have the latest version of the Facebook app installed. If not, update it through Google Play Store before proceeding further. **Step 2:** Scroll down until you find the option called **"Friends"**. It should be under the heading "Explore". Click on this option. Guideline: The 'Friends' section provides various ways to manage your friend list such as recently interacted with friends, birthdays, etc., but what we need here is the "Create New List" function which can be found by clicking on the blue button with a plus sign (+). **Step 3:** After tapping on the "+ Create" button, two new options appear - "New Friend List" and "Close Friends". Choose **"New Friend List"**. In Depth Explanation: Choosing "New Friend List" lets you create a custom category according to your preference. On the other hand, selecting "Close Friends" automatically adds certain individuals whom Facebook thinks you are closest to based on interactions like comments, likes, shares, etc. **Step 4:** Now, name your new list appropriately. For instance, if you want to categorize your high school mates, type "High School Buddies". Then press "Save". Key Point: Naming conventions help maintain organization and ease of understanding when dealing with multiple lists. Be descriptive yet concise while naming your lists. **Step 5:** Once saved, go back to your news feed page. To add friends to this newly created list, tap again on the hamburger menu > Friends > Your Newly Created List. Remember: You won't see any names within the list initially because no one has been added yet. **Step 6:** Inside your empty list, hit the "Add People" button. A search bar pops up along with suggested friends based on your activity history. Note: These suggestions might include mutual friends, frequent interaction partners, or those who also belong to similar groups. **Step 7:** Type the name(s) of the friend(s) you wish to add in the search box provided. As soon as their profile shows up below the search bar, tick the circle next to their name(s). Did You Know? Adding someone to a Custom List does NOT remove them from your Overall Friend Count! **Step 8:** Having selected all intended recipients, click on "Done" located at the upper right corner. Congratulations! You just successfully grouped some of your Facebook friends together! To confirm, navigate to your newly made list. There you should see the faces of everyone included. Repeat these steps whenever necessary to sort out more circles of pals! By applying this method consistently over time, managing privacy settings, post visibility, and general social media experience becomes much simpler. Happy organizing! Hi Josh, Imogen, Leila, Sam, Alycia and Obi, The colour of the Moon is very subtle: it has soft shades of grey, black and white, and regions of browns, blues and yellows. We generally say there are four phases of the Moon. New, First Quarter, Full, and Last Quarter. See the Moon Phases page of our website. It is actually possible to see the whole Moon during the crescent moon phase. Sometimes there is so much light reflected from the Earth (Earthshine), that it lights up the part of the Moon that is not lit up by the Sun: we see the bright crescent Moon as well as the pale remainder of the Moon. As the Moon phase moves towards the Full moon, the Moon is so bright you can no longer see the arthshine on the Moon. The Moon has to be in the right position for the Earth to reflect the Sun's light onto the Moon's night side; this only happens just after the New Moon. This is called the Da Vinci Glow. You can find out more about it on the NASA website. The diameter (width) of the moon is 3475.6 km (0.272 x that of Earth). The moon is (on average) 384 400 km from the Earth. The Moon does not have a moon of its own. You can learn more about The Moon on our website. So that would be we just listen to it. You don't even let me build it. [gasp] XXX look at this book you can touch and feel. All Kenyans of school-going age are required to attend Primary School. However, school fees and required uniforms often keep students away from school. The Kenyan school system consists of 8 years of primary school, standard 1 through 8, 4 years of high school (Form 1 to 4) and 4 years of university but plans are underway of changing the system to 2 years in pre-school, 6 years in primary school,3 years in junior high school,3 years in senior high school and 3 years in university (2-6-6-3) in 2018. At the end of primary school, all students sit for a standardized exam called Kenya Certificate of Primary Education (KCPE). The grades attained in this exam determine which high school the student will attend. After learning about triads and how we use them to "build" chords, most of the other stuff is pretty mundane, which is probably why it causes no end of argument among people. Today we'll look at how chords other than our four basic ones (major, minor, augmented and diminished) are created. As we will be taking time later this year to examine many of these chords individually, you can consider today an "introduction," if you will. In the case of some of the chords, it will actually be more of a second visitation! But first, last week's answers: - a) major - a) C#, E, G# b) F#, A#, C# c) E, G#, C (or B#, if you prefer) d) A, C, Eb e) G, B, D# f) C, E, G# g) B, D#, F# h) Bb, Db, F i) D, F, A j) F, Ab, B (or Cb, if you prefer) k) G#, C, D# l) Eb, G, Bb m) C, Eb, Gb Taking The Fifth Hate to do this to you good folks, but we'll need to have a scale with which to form some examples and (well, you know the drill by now!) As we've discussed before, a chord formed from a triad will consist of the I, III and V of any given scale. As we learned last week, the particular intervals of the third (that is, whether they be major or minor thirds) are what will determine which of our four basic chords we have. In the case of the major and the minor chords (being the two we use most frequently), the "key" note is the third. Since the root and the fifth are the same in any given major or minor chord, this makes perfect sense. In a C major chord, the three notes are C, E and G. In C minor, the notes are C, Eb and G. In the article Happy New Ear, we went over this pretty well. There is quite a distinction between the sound of a major chord and its namesake minor. And this is a good thing because we usually define the tonality of any given piece of music in terms of its key. Teach Your Children is in D major. Have A Cigar is in E minor. This sense of tonality is, not to put too much importance on it or anything, fairly vital to how we learn to play music, write music and analyze music. The guitar, like many polyphonic instruments (any instrument capable of sounding two or more separate notes at a time), is quite adept at masking tonality. In Scales Within Scales we briefly touched upon what I called "neutered chords." These are chords that are made up of just the roots and fifths - there are no thirds. These are the mainstay of many a rock or metal guitarist. Here are a few examples: I've always found it incredibly amusing that the "common" name given to these ambiguous voicings is "power chords." Now there's a term of modern arrogance if there ever was one Technically, writers will designate these chords as "whatever 5 or "such and such (no 3rd)." I'm sure you've all run into these before. Up In The Air Something else you've all run into before (and we discussed in Multiple Personality Disorder) is the "suspended chord." A suspended chord is created when we replace the third in the triad with the fourth. Occasionally one may use the second and sometimes people use both. Here's the example with which the majority of you are most familiar: Here the third (F#) is replaced by either the fourth (the G in the Dsus4) or the second (the E in the Dsus2). This chord is typically used in passing, going from one point to another. It rarely serves any other purpose than to just liven things up a bit or supporting a melody line. It has become standard practice to assume that any chord marked "sus" refers to the use of the fourth, but as we'll later discuss, that's not always a valid assumption. By the way, here's your first homework problem and I'll admit it's not truly a fair question. Here are five suspended chords taken right from a standard guitar chord chart. And I'll pretty much guarantee that these are how most of us learned them. However, two of them are actually not suspended chords, at least not by the definition in virtually every textbook on theory. Which two are they? For those of you who picked C and G, congratulations. No more homework for you this week! For those of you who didn't, the key to the definition is that the fourth replaces the third. Both the C and G chords carry the third on two separate strings. In our examples the Csus has an E note on the open E (1st) string, while the Gsus sports a B note on the second fret of the A (5th) string. But, just to prove I'm a good sport, no homework for you either. As I said, it was a tricky question. And it gets even trickier as we move onward. Sixes And Sevens And Nines (oh my!) Most of the other chords you might encounter involving adding an additional note (or notes) to your basic triad. This means, obviously, that you will have a chord made up of at least four, and possibly more, notes. First, Let's expand our C major scale a bit: The sixth chord of any key is created by adding the note at the sixth interval from the root to the entire triad. You are not replacing anything, you are building onto the original structure. If we look at our C major scale, you can see that a C6 chord would consist of C, E, G and A. Similarly, a G6 would have the G, B, D and E notes. Seventh chords we covered fairly well in Happy New Ear. If there's any real trick to them, it's in remembering that whenever you see any chord with just the "7 designation, it means to add the minor seventh interval. Only use the major seventh when it specifically calls for it by name with the "maj7 designation. When we get into ninths, elevenths and thirteenths, things begin to get both interesting and confusing at the same time. For the sake of not overworking our already overworked editor (and esteemed webmaster), I'm going to gloss over these for the time being. The most important thing for you to remember right now is that each of these chords is dependent upon its predecessor for its creation. You cannot have a ninth without using the seventh (and the minor seventh, at that), you cannot have the eleventh without both the seventh and the ninth and so on. If you look at this in notation, it might make a little more sense: You can see why I called them "stackables" last time out. All you have to do is keep adding on every other note until you run out of space Another important thing to note is that the "m" designation for "minor" (should) always refer to the original triad and not to the numbered interval. Thus an Am9, for example, would be your A minor triad (A, C, E) with the minor 7th (G) and finally the 9th (B) tacked on. Finally, you can also simply "add" a note. Really. C (add9) would be C, E, G and D. Note that this is different from Csus2 in that the D does not replace the third (E). As the name states, we simply throw another note (or however many are named) onto the pile. The Name Game And this is how it all becomes very confusing. Here are four notes - A, C, E and G. What chord is it? The way I've written it out might make you think that it is an Am7. That certainly works. But how about C6? We've already done that one, haven't we? C, E, G and A. Yes, that works as well. But we can also go overboard - say, Gsus4sus2 (add 6). You're right, there's no D to serve as the fifth, but we've seen notes dropped out of chords before. We could always call it Gsus4sus2 (no 5)(add 6). Not to mince around or anything The point is that a big part of what a chord is named depends on what it is being used for. You may not know it, but context is a vital part of any chord. I've told you before but it really needs repeating. Music is motion. Melodies and chord progressions move from one point to another and that movement helps us to define matters. Musicians and songwriters should be constantly aware of the purpose of a chord pattern, of the function that a particular chord can serve in a song. "Sounding good" is always nice, but believe it or not there are reasons why certain progressions "sound good." We will be examining all of our chords individually at some point this fall/winter. This will include using them in songwriting exercises as well as looking at how they have been used in the past by other songwriters. I would also recommend that, in your spare time, you pick a chord, any chord, and write out its component notes. You're riding your car/bus/train to work/school/home and you either do it in your head or on a piece of paper (but not if you're driving!!!). This kind of practice will help you immeasurably, especially when we get to working on scales and leads. As always, feel free to write me with any questions, comments, concerns or topics that you'd like to see covered in the future. Title: How to Perform the "Reject" Dance Move The "Reject" is a popular dance move that has been performed by various celebrities and dancers around the world. It involves quickly pushing away an imaginary object or person with one hand while simultaneously brushing off the shoulder with the other hand. This move can be used in a variety of settings, from social gatherings to professional performances. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do the reject like a pro. **Step 1: Get into Position** Start by standing upright with your feet hip-width apart. Make sure you have enough space around you to perform the move without bumping into anything or anyone. Keep your arms relaxed at your sides. Key Tip: Maintain good posture throughout the entire movement. A strong core will help you execute the move more smoothly. **Step 2: Push Away with One Hand** Imagine there's an invisible force or person directly in front of you. Quickly extend one arm forward as if you're pushing something away. Your elbow should remain slightly bent during this motion. The power comes from your hips and shoulders rather than just your arm. Guideline: Choose which arm you want to lead with based on comfort and preference. If you're right-handed, you might find it easier to start with your right arm; left-handers may prefer their left arm. **Step 3: Brush Off the Shoulder** As soon as you push away with one hand, immediately bring the opposite hand up to brush off your shoulder. This action signifies dismissal or rejection of whatever was pushed away. Both movements (pushing and brushing) should happen almost simultaneously. Key Tip: Use a flicking motion when brushing off the shoulder. Think about shaking off water droplets after washing your hands. **Step 4: Add Flair** To make the reject look even cooler, add some flair to your body language. You could puff out your chest slightly as you push away, snap your fingers along with the beat, or twist your torso slightly before returning to neutral position. These extra touches are what set great dancers apart! Guideline: Practice different variations until you find one that feels natural and looks impressive. Remember, confidence is key when dancing. **Step 5: Combine with Other Steps** Once you feel comfortable doing the reject on its own, try incorporating it into other dance moves or routines. For example, you could do the reject after performing a spin or jump, adding emphasis to those moves. Or, you could chain several repeats of the reject together, creating a rhythmic pattern that flows seamlessly within the music. Key Tip: Always listen to the song and let the rhythm guide your movements. Dancing should feel fluid and organic, not forced or robotic. **Step 6: Watch and Learn** Study videos of professionals doing the reject to gain inspiration and refine your technique. Pay attention to their footwork, arm placement, and overall body control. Don't be afraid to mimic their movements closely - imitation is often the best form of learning. Guideline: Be patient with yourself. Mastering any new skill takes time and dedication. Keep practicing, stay positive, and most importantly, enjoy the process! He argued that todays continents once formed a single landmass, which he named Pangaea (Greek for "all land"). It broke into pieces due to the weaknesses in the earth's crust as they were made up of less dense materials, which drifted centimeter by centimeter over millions of years until they arrived at where they are now. Figure 1.5 shows how the Pangaea split up into plates and drifted over the millions of years. (Fig 1.5) The plates drifted through the years to where they are now. It is possible for the plates to move due to the convection currents in the mantle. The movement of these currents is similar to the movement of convection currents in a beaker of boiling water. (Fig 1.6) Convection currents and plate movements. (Fig 1.7) Magma heats up and rises. Water that is heated expands and rises to the surface of the beaker. Similarly, the magma nearer the core expands and rises. (Fig 1.8) Magma spreads out, plates move apart. Water that has risen spreads out across the surface, cools and sinks to the bottom. Similarly, the magma that has risen spreads out beneath the plates. As the magma spreads out, the plates are dragged along and they move away from each other. This is the process of sea-floor spreading. (Fig 1.9) Magma sinks, plates dragged towards each other. When the magma sinks, the plates are dragged towards each other. This is the process of subduction. The repeated heating and rising of the magma sets up continuous convection currents in the mantle, causing the plates to move. This is where CollabShot comes in - a web-based tool that simplifies the process by enabling real-time collaboration on screenshots. Here's an in-depth guide on how to collaborate on screenshots with CollabShot: **Step 1:** Open your preferred web browser and navigate to <https://www.collashot.com/>. This will take you to the CollabShot homepage. _Key tip:_ Make sure you have a stable internet connection to ensure seamless collaboration. **Step 2:** Click on the "Get Started" button located at the top right corner of the page. You will be redirected to a signup form. _Guideline:_ If you already have an account, click on the "Sign In" button instead and proceed to Step 4. **Step 3:** Fill out the required fields (name, email address, and password) and then click on the "Create Account" button. An activation link will be sent to your email; click on it to activate your account. _Key tip:_ Use a secure password containing letters, numbers, and special characters to protect your account from unauthorized access. **Step 4:** After activating your account, log in using your newly created credentials. Once logged in, you will see two options - "Upload Image" and "New Session." Choose "New Session" to start a new collaboration session. _Guideline:_ A new session creates a unique URL that you can share with others to collaborate on the same set of screenshots. **Step 5:** Upload the first screenshot you want to collaborate on by clicking on the "+ Add Screen" button. Select the image file from your device and wait for it to upload. _Key tip:_ To maintain context during collaboration, consider naming the screenshot appropriately before uploading it. **Step 6:** Once the screenshot has been uploaded, invite others to join the collaboration session by sharing the generated URL with them via email, chat, or any other communication platform. _Guideline:_ Recipients do not need a CollabShot account to view and contribute to the session; they only require the unique URL. **Step 7:** As participants join the session, they can add comments, drawings, and highlights directly onto the screenshot. They may also upload additional screenshots related to the topic being discussed. _Key tip:_ Encourage clear and concise feedback to streamline the collaboration process. **Step 8:** Review and respond to the comments, drawings, and highlights added by other participants. Resolve issues, answer questions, and provide guidance where necessary. _Guideline:_ Keep track of all changes made during the collaboration session to ensure everyone stays up-to-date. **Step 9:** When all feedback has been addressed and resolved, close the collaboration session by clicking on the "End Session" button at the bottom left corner of the screen. _Key tip:_ Save a copy of the finalized screenshots for future reference or further editing. By following these steps, you can effectively collaborate on screenshots using CollabShot. The tool offers numerous benefits, including increased efficiency, improved organization, and enhanced teamwork. Happy collaborating! How do you know he said it? Can you put them up on the table? He has a big bag of mail. "Hello, Ben and Mia. I have some mail for you," the mailman says, waving. "Mail for us? Wow!" Ben and Mia say, running to the gate. The mailman gives them each a colorful envelope. They have stickers and drawings on them. "Who are they from?" Ben asks, curious. "They are from your grandma and grandpa. They live far away, but they love you very much. They wrote you some letters and sent you some pictures," the mailman explains. "Thank you, mailman. You are very nice," Mia says, grateful. "You're welcome, kids. Have fun reading your mail. And be careful with the scissors," the mailman says, leaving. Ben and Mia run back to their table. They open their envelopes with scissors. They find letters and pictures inside. They see their grandma and grandpa smiling and waving. They read their letters out loud. They say they miss them and they are proud of them. They also send them some jokes and riddles. "Grandma and grandpa are very funny," Ben says, laughing. "They are also very serious.
